- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

6	//! CUDA module and function management (RAII, PTX/cubin loading).
7	//!
8	//! A [`CudaModule`] wraps a `CUmodule` loaded from PTX source or a cubin file.
9	//! [`CudaFunction`] extracts a kernel entry point from a loaded module by
10	//! symbol name. Both types are reference-counted and tie their lifetime to the
11	//! parent [`CudaContext`] / [`CudaModule`] respectively.
12	//!
13	//! # Typical workflow
14	//!
15	//! ```ignore
16	//! let ctx = CudaContext::new(0)?;
17	//! let module = ctx.load_module_from_ptx_src(ptx)?;
18	//! let kernel = module.load_function("my_kernel")?;
19	//! ```
20	//!
21	//! # Raw CUDA interop
22	//!
23	//! Most users should load kernels with [`CudaModule::load_function`] and
24	//! launch them through cuda-oxide's typed launch helpers. Some CUDA-adjacent
25	//! libraries need the underlying driver handle to inspect or register
26	//! module-scope device state before launch. For those cases,
27	//! [`CudaModule::cu_module`] exposes a non-owning raw `CUmodule` handle under
28	//! an explicit `unsafe` contract.
29	
30	use crate::context::CudaContext;
31	use crate::error::{DriverError, IntoResult};
32	use std::borrow::Cow;
33	use std::ffi::{CString, c_void};
34	use std::mem::MaybeUninit;
35	use std::sync::Arc;
36	
37	/// An RAII wrapper around a `CUmodule` handle.
38	///
39	/// Holds an `Arc<CudaContext>` to ensure the context outlives the module.
40	/// Unloaded automatically via `cuModuleUnload` on [`Drop`].
41	#[derive(Debug)]
42	pub struct CudaModule {
43	    /// Raw CUDA module handle.
44	    pub(crate) cu_module: cuda_bindings::CUmodule,
45	    /// Owning context. Kept alive for the lifetime of this module.
46	    pub(crate) ctx: Arc<CudaContext>,
47	}
48	
49	/// # Safety
50	///
51	/// `CUmodule` handles are not thread-local. The CUDA driver permits querying
52	/// functions from a module on any thread, provided the owning context is bound.
53	unsafe impl Send for CudaModule {}
54	/// See [`Send`] impl.
55	unsafe impl Sync for CudaModule {}
56	
57	/// Unloads the module on drop.
58	///
59	/// Binds the context to the current thread first (required by
60	/// `cuModuleUnload`). Errors are recorded on the context rather than
61	/// panicking.
62	impl Drop for CudaModule {
63	    fn drop(&mut self) {
64	        self.ctx.record_err(self.ctx.bind_to_thread());
65	        self.ctx
66	            .record_err(unsafe { cuda_bindings::cuModuleUnload(self.cu_module).result() });
67	    }
68	}
69	
70	impl CudaContext {
71	    /// JIT-compiles PTX source and loads the resulting module into this
72	    /// context.
73	    ///
74	    /// `ptx_src` must be a valid, null-terminator-free PTX string. The driver
75	    /// performs JIT compilation targeting the current device architecture.
76	    ///
77	    /// # Panics
78	    ///
79	    /// Panics if `ptx_src` contains interior null bytes.
80	    pub fn load_module_from_ptx_src(
81	        self: &Arc<Self>,
82	        ptx_src: &str,
83	    ) -> Result<Arc<CudaModule>, DriverError> {
84	        self.bind_to_thread()?;
85	        let c_src = CString::new(ptx_src).unwrap();
86	        let cu_module = unsafe {
87	            let mut cu_module = MaybeUninit::uninit();
88	            cuda_bindings::cuModuleLoadData(cu_module.as_mut_ptr(), c_src.as_ptr() as *const _)
89	                .result()?;
90	            cu_module.assume_init()
91	        };
92	        Ok(Arc::new(CudaModule {
93	            cu_module,
94	            ctx: self.clone(),
95	        }))
96	    }
97	
98	    /// Loads a CUDA module from an in-memory image.
99	    ///
100	    /// `image` may be PTX source bytes, a cubin, or a fatbin. PTX text is
101	    /// null-terminated before it is passed to the CUDA driver; binary module
102	    /// images tolerate the trailing byte because their own headers describe
103	    /// their size.
104	    pub fn load_module_from_image(
105	        self: &Arc<Self>,
106	        image: &[u8],
107	    ) -> Result<Arc<CudaModule>, DriverError> {
108	        self.bind_to_thread()?;
109	        let image = null_terminated_image(image);
110	        let cu_module = unsafe {
111	            let mut cu_module = MaybeUninit::uninit();
112	            cuda_bindings::cuModuleLoadData(cu_module.as_mut_ptr(), image.as_ptr() as *const _)
113	                .result()?;
114	            cu_module.assume_init()
115	        };
116	        Ok(Arc::new(CudaModule {
117	            cu_module,
118	            ctx: self.clone(),
119	        }))
120	    }
121	
122	    /// Loads a module from a cubin or PTX file on disk.
123	    ///
124	    /// `filename` is the filesystem path. The driver selects the loader based
125	    /// on file contents (PTX text or cubin ELF).
126	    ///
127	    /// # Panics
128	    ///
129	    /// Panics if `filename` contains interior null bytes.
130	    pub fn load_module_from_file(
131	        self: &Arc<Self>,
132	        filename: &str,
133	    ) -> Result<Arc<CudaModule>, DriverError> {
134	        self.bind_to_thread()?;
135	        let c_str = CString::new(filename).unwrap();
136	        let mut cu_module = MaybeUninit::uninit();
137	        let cu_module = unsafe {
138	            cuda_bindings::cuModuleLoad(cu_module.as_mut_ptr(), c_str.as_ptr()).result()?;
139	            cu_module.assume_init()
140	        };
141	        Ok(Arc::new(CudaModule {
142	            cu_module,
143	            ctx: self.clone(),
144	        }))
145	    }
146	}
147	
148	fn null_terminated_image(image: &[u8]) -> Cow<'_, [u8]> {
149	    if image.last() == Some(&0) {
150	        Cow::Borrowed(image)
151	    } else {
152	        let mut owned = Vec::with_capacity(image.len() + 1);
153	        owned.extend_from_slice(image);
154	        owned.push(0);
155	        Cow::Owned(owned)
156	    }
157	}
158	
159	/// A handle to a device kernel entry point within a loaded [`CudaModule`].
160	///
161	/// Holds an `Arc<CudaModule>` so the module (and transitively the context)
162	/// remains loaded for the lifetime of this handle. Cloning is cheap (just an
163	/// `Arc` bump).
164	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
165	pub struct CudaFunction {
166	    /// Raw CUDA function handle.
167	    pub(crate) cu_function: cuda_bindings::CUfunction,
168	    /// Owning module. Prevents unloading while this function handle exists.
169	    #[allow(unused)]
170	    pub(crate) module: Arc<CudaModule>,
171	}
172	
173	/// # Safety
174	///
175	/// `CUfunction` handles are derived from a `CUmodule` and valid in any thread
176	/// that has the owning context bound.
177	unsafe impl Send for CudaFunction {}
178	/// See [`Send`] impl.
179	unsafe impl Sync for CudaFunction {}
180	
181	impl CudaModule {
182	    /// Returns the parent [`CudaContext`].
183	    ///
184	    /// This is mainly useful when interoperating with raw CUDA driver APIs:
185	    /// call [`CudaContext::bind_to_thread`] on this context before passing raw
186	    /// module/function handles to APIs that require the owning context to be
187	    /// current on the calling host thread.
188	    pub fn context(&self) -> &Arc<CudaContext> {
189	        &self.ctx
190	    }
191	
192	    /// Returns the raw `CUmodule` handle owned by this wrapper.
193	    ///
194	    /// This is an escape hatch for CUDA driver interop libraries that need to
195	    /// inspect or register a loaded module directly. For example, NVSHMEM uses
196	    /// module-level state and may need the raw `CUmodule` before launching a
197	    /// kernel that calls into NVSHMEM device code.
198	    ///
199	    /// The returned handle is copied by value, but it is non-owning.
200	    /// cuda-oxide still owns the module and will unload it when the last
201	    /// [`Arc`] owning this module is dropped.
202	    ///
203	    /// # Safety
204	    ///
205	    /// - The returned handle is valid only while this [`CudaModule`] remains
206	    ///   alive. If the handle is stored outside the immediate call, the caller
207	    ///   must keep an [`Arc`] to this module alive for at least as long.
208	    /// - The caller must not unload the module through the raw handle, transfer
209	    ///   ownership of it, or pass it to any API that may invalidate module,
210	    ///   function, or global handles owned by cuda-oxide.
211	    /// - Before passing the handle to CUDA driver APIs or interop libraries
212	    ///   that make driver calls, the caller must ensure this module's owning
213	    ///   context is current on the calling host thread, for example with
214	    ///   [`CudaModule::context`] followed by
215	    ///   [`CudaContext::bind_to_thread`].
216	    /// - Any foreign library that retains this handle must obey the same
217	    ///   lifetime and context-current requirements. cuda-oxide cannot enforce
218	    ///   those requirements once the raw handle leaves Rust's type system.
219	    pub unsafe fn cu_module(&self) -> cuda_bindings::CUmodule {
220	        self.cu_module
221	    }
222	
223	    /// Looks up a kernel entry point by `fn_name` in this module.
224	    ///
225	    /// The returned [`CudaFunction`] holds an `Arc` back to this module,
226	    /// preventing unloading while the handle is live.
227	    ///
228	    /// This method first binds the module's owning context to the calling
229	    /// thread, then performs `cuModuleGetFunction`. That makes it safe to look
230	    /// up functions from any host thread, provided the module and its context
231	    /// are still alive.
232	    ///
233	    /// # Errors
234	    ///
235	    /// Returns an error if binding the module's context fails or if
236	    /// `cuModuleGetFunction` cannot resolve `fn_name` in this module.
237	    ///
238	    /// # Panics
239	    ///
240	    /// Panics if `fn_name` contains interior null bytes.
241	    pub fn load_function(self: &Arc<Self>, fn_name: &str) -> Result<CudaFunction, DriverError> {
242	        self.ctx.bind_to_thread()?;
243	        let c_name = CString::new(fn_name).unwrap();
244	        let cu_function = unsafe {
245	            let mut cu_function = MaybeUninit::uninit();
246	            cuda_bindings::cuModuleGetFunction(
247	                cu_function.as_mut_ptr(),
248	                self.cu_module,
249	                c_name.as_ptr(),
250	            )
251	            .result()?;
252	            cu_function.assume_init()
253	        };
254	        Ok(CudaFunction {
255	            cu_function,
256	            module: self.clone(),
257	        })
258	    }
259	}
260	
261	/// A resolved handle to a `#[constant]` device global. Macro-generated
262	/// `set_<name>` methods resolve these lazily on first use and cache the
263	/// handle on the `LoadedModule` struct. Callers pass `size_of::<T>()` on
264	/// every write; correctness depends on the resolver asserting that the
265	/// driver-reported size matches the host-side type.
266	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]
267	pub struct ConstantHandle {
268	    pub(crate) dptr: cuda_bindings::CUdeviceptr,
269	}
270	
271	impl ConstantHandle {
272	    /// Construct from a raw device pointer. Used by macro-generated
273	    /// `LoadedModule` initializers after [`CudaModule::get_global`] has
274	    /// resolved the symbol and the size has been asserted against
275	    /// `size_of::<T>()`.
276	    ///
277	    /// # Safety
278	    ///
279	    /// `dptr` must point to at least `size_of::<T>()` bytes of constant
280	    /// memory in a still-loaded module.
281	    pub unsafe fn from_raw(dptr: cuda_bindings::CUdeviceptr) -> Self {
282	        Self { dptr }
283	    }
284	}
285	
286	impl ConstantHandle {
287	    /// Stream-ordered `cuMemcpyHtoDAsync` from `src` (`num_bytes` of host
288	    /// memory) into the device global.
289	    ///
290	    /// # Safety
291	    ///
292	    /// - `src` must point to at least `num_bytes` of readable host memory.
293	    /// - The bytes must have a layout compatible with the device-side type.
294	    pub unsafe fn write_async(
295	        &self,
296	        stream: &crate::CudaStream,
297	        src: *const u8,
298	        num_bytes: usize,
299	    ) -> Result<(), DriverError> {
300	        stream.context().bind_to_thread()?;
301	        unsafe { crate::memory::memcpy_htod_async(self.dptr, src, num_bytes, stream.cu_stream()) }
302	    }
303	
304	    /// Stream-ordered `cuMemcpyHtoDAsync` from owned host bytes into the
305	    /// device global.
306	    ///
307	    /// The bytes are kept alive until the stream reaches a host callback
308	    /// enqueued after the copy. This makes safe setters sound even when the
309	    /// caller passes a temporary such as `&3.0`.
310	    pub fn write_async_staged(
311	        &self,
312	        stream: &crate::CudaStream,
313	        bytes: Box<[MaybeUninit<u8>]>,
314	    ) -> Result<(), DriverError> {
315	        stream.context().bind_to_thread()?;
316	        let num_bytes = bytes.len();
317	        if num_bytes == 0 {
318	            return Ok(());
319	        }
320	
321	        unsafe {
322	            crate::memory::memcpy_htod_async(
323	                self.dptr,
324	                bytes.as_ptr() as *const u8,
325	                num_bytes,
326	                stream.cu_stream(),
327	            )?;
328	        }
329	
330	        unsafe extern "C" fn drop_staged_bytes(callback: *mut c_void) {
331	            drop(unsafe {
332	                Box::<Box<[MaybeUninit<u8>]>>::from_raw(callback as *mut Box<[MaybeUninit<u8>]>)
333	            });
334	        }
335	
336	        let callback_data = Box::into_raw(Box::new(bytes)) as *mut c_void;
337	        let callback_result = unsafe {
338	            cuda_bindings::cuLaunchHostFunc(
339	                stream.cu_stream(),
340	                Some(drop_staged_bytes),
341	                callback_data,
342	            )
343	        }
344	        .result();
345	
346	        if let Err(err) = callback_result {
347	            let staged = unsafe {
348	                Box::<Box<[MaybeUninit<u8>]>>::from_raw(
349	                    callback_data as *mut Box<[MaybeUninit<u8>]>,
350	                )
351	            };
352	            if let Err(sync_err) = stream.synchronize() {
353	                Box::leak(staged);
354	                return Err(sync_err);
355	            }
356	            drop(staged);
357	            return Err(err);
358	        }
359	
360	        Ok(())
361	    }
362	
363	    /// Synchronous `cuMemcpyHtoD` from `src` into the device global. Blocks
364	    /// the calling thread.
365	    ///
366	    /// # Safety
367	    ///
368	    /// Same contract as [`write_async`](Self::write_async).
369	    pub unsafe fn write_blocking(
370	        &self,
371	        module: &Arc<CudaModule>,
372	        src: *const u8,
373	        num_bytes: usize,
374	    ) -> Result<(), DriverError> {
375	        unsafe { module.copy_bytes_to_device_global_sync(self.dptr, src, num_bytes) }
376	    }
377	}
378	
379	impl CudaModule {
380	    /// Resolves a device global by name and returns its device pointer and
381	    /// size in bytes.
382	    ///
383	    /// Used to find `__constant__`-style globals (and other module-scope
384	    /// device symbols) so the host can populate them via `cuMemcpyHtoD`.
385	    /// The returned size is what the driver recorded for the symbol — host
386	    /// code should assert it matches the expected element size before
387	    /// copying.
388	    ///
389	    /// Binds the owning context to the calling thread first.
390	    ///
391	    /// # Errors
392	    ///
393	    /// Returns an error if binding fails or if `name` cannot be resolved
394	    /// in this module.
395	    ///
396	    /// # Panics
397	    ///
398	    /// Panics if `name` contains interior null bytes.
399	    pub fn get_global(
400	        self: &Arc<Self>,
401	        name: &str,
402	    ) -> Result<(cuda_bindings::CUdeviceptr, usize), DriverError> {
403	        self.ctx.bind_to_thread()?;
404	        let c_name = CString::new(name).unwrap();
405	        let mut dptr = MaybeUninit::<cuda_bindings::CUdeviceptr>::uninit();
406	        let mut size = MaybeUninit::<usize>::uninit();
407	        unsafe {
408	            cuda_bindings::cuModuleGetGlobal_v2(
409	                dptr.as_mut_ptr(),
410	                size.as_mut_ptr(),
411	                self.cu_module,
412	                c_name.as_ptr(),
413	            )
414	            .result()?;
415	            Ok((dptr.assume_init(), size.assume_init()))
416	        }
417	    }
418	
419	    /// Synchronously copies `num_bytes` from host memory at `src` into the
420	    /// device global at `dptr`.
421	    ///
422	    /// Intended for populating `#[constant]` statics from the macro-generated
423	    /// `set_<name>_blocking` methods on `LoadedModule`. The caller is expected to have
424	    /// already resolved `dptr` via [`get_global`](Self::get_global) and
425	    /// verified that the driver-reported size matches the host type's size.
426	    ///
427	    /// # Safety
428	    ///
429	    /// - `dptr` must be a valid device pointer with at least `num_bytes` of
430	    ///   accessible storage.
431	    /// - `src` must point to at least `num_bytes` of readable host memory.
432	    /// - The device-side type at `dptr` and the host bytes at `src` must
433	    ///   have compatible layout.
434	    pub unsafe fn copy_bytes_to_device_global_sync(
435	        self: &Arc<Self>,
436	        dptr: cuda_bindings::CUdeviceptr,
437	        src: *const u8,
438	        num_bytes: usize,
439	    ) -> Result<(), DriverError> {
440	        self.ctx.bind_to_thread()?;
441	        unsafe { crate::memory::memcpy_htod_sync(dptr, src, num_bytes) }
442	    }
443	}
444	
445	impl CudaFunction {
446	    /// Returns the raw `CUfunction` handle.
447	    ///
448	    /// # Safety
449	    ///
450	    /// The returned handle is copied by value, but it is non-owning. It is
451	    /// invalidated if the parent [`CudaModule`] is dropped.
452	    ///
453	    /// Because [`CudaFunction`] holds an [`Arc`] to its parent module, the
454	    /// module cannot be unloaded while `self` is alive. If the raw handle is
455	    /// stored outside the immediate call, the caller must keep this
456	    /// [`CudaFunction`] or another [`Arc`] owning the parent module alive for
457	    /// at least as long as the raw handle is used.
458	    pub unsafe fn cu_function(&self) -> cuda_bindings::CUfunction {
459	        self.cu_function
460	    }
461	}

6	//! Kernel launch configuration.
7	//!
8	//! [`LaunchConfig`] bundles the grid dimensions, block dimensions, and dynamic
9	//! shared memory size required by [`launch_kernel`](crate::launch_kernel). Use
10	//! [`LaunchConfig::for_num_elems`] as a quick 1-D launch helper.
11	
12	/// Grid and block dimensions plus dynamic shared memory size for a kernel
13	/// launch.
14	///
15	/// Each dimension tuple is `(x, y, z)`. Pass this to
16	/// [`launch_kernel`](crate::launch_kernel) or destructure it manually for
17	/// custom launch wrappers.
18	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]
19	pub struct LaunchConfig {
20	    /// Grid dimensions `(x, y, z)` in blocks.
21	    pub grid_dim: (u32, u32, u32),
22	    /// Block dimensions `(x, y, z)` in threads.
23	    pub block_dim: (u32, u32, u32),
24	    /// Bytes of dynamic shared memory allocated per block.
25	    pub shared_mem_bytes: u32,
26	}
27	
28	impl LaunchConfig {
29	    /// Creates a 1-D launch configuration for `n` elements.
30	    ///
31	    /// Uses a block size of 256 threads and computes the grid size via
32	    /// ceiling division. No dynamic shared memory is requested.
33	    ///
34	    /// Suitable for simple element-wise kernels where thread index maps
35	    /// directly to element index.
36	    pub fn for_num_elems(n: u32) -> Self {
37	        const DEFAULT_BLOCK_SIZE: u32 = 256;
38	        let grid_x = n.div_ceil(DEFAULT_BLOCK_SIZE);
39	        LaunchConfig {
40	            grid_dim: (grid_x, 1, 1),
41	            block_dim: (DEFAULT_BLOCK_SIZE, 1, 1),
42	            shared_mem_bytes: 0,
43	        }
44	    }
45	}

6	//! Safe RAII wrappers around the CUDA driver API.
7	//!
8	//! This crate provides Rust-idiomatic, RAII-managed wrappers over the low-level CUDA
9	//! driver API (`cuInit`, `cuCtx*`, `cuStream*`, `cuEvent*`, `cuModule*`, `cuMem*`).
10	//! All GPU resources are released automatically on [`Drop`], and error codes are
11	//! propagated as [`DriverError`] values.
12	//!
13	//! # Architecture
14	//!
15	//! - [`CudaContext`] -- retains the device primary context and tracks per-context
16	//!   state (stream count, error accumulation).
17	//! - [`CudaStream`] -- non-blocking stream with fork/join parallelism and host
18	//!   callback support for bridging to async Rust.
19	//! - [`CudaEvent`] -- lightweight synchronization primitive for inter-stream
20	//!   ordering and elapsed-time measurement.
21	//! - [`CudaModule`] / [`CudaFunction`] -- PTX/cubin loading and kernel handle
22	//!   extraction.
23	//! - [`PinnedHostBuffer`] -- page-locked host memory for CUDA transfers.
24	//! - [`LaunchConfig`] -- grid/block dimension helper.
25	//! - [`memory`] -- free functions for device allocation, transfer, and memset
26	//!   (both stream-ordered async and synchronous variants).
27	//!
28	//! # Context binding
29	//!
30	//! Most driver calls require a CUDA context bound to the calling thread. Methods
31	//! on the wrapper types call [`CudaContext::bind_to_thread`] internally so the
32	//! caller does not need to manage `cuCtxSetCurrent` manually.
33	//!
34	//! # Re-exports
35	//!
36	//! Raw bindings are available as [`sys`] for any driver entry point not yet
37	//! wrapped.
38

83	/// Initializes the CUDA driver API. Must be called before any other driver API
84	/// function. Safe to call multiple times; subsequent calls are no-ops.
85	///
86	/// `flags` is reserved by CUDA and must currently be `0`.
87	///
88	/// [`CudaContext::new`] calls this automatically, so explicit invocation is only
89	/// needed when using raw bindings via [`sys`].
90	///
91	/// # Safety
92	///
93	/// - A CUDA-capable device and a compatible driver must be installed.
94	/// - Must not be called from a GPU callback (`cuLaunchHostFunc`).
95	pub unsafe fn init(flags: c_uint) -> Result<(), DriverError> {
96	    unsafe { cuda_bindings::cuInit(flags) }.result()

99	/// Low-level wrapper around `cuLaunchKernel`.
100	///
101	/// The launch is **asynchronous** with respect to the host: this function
102	/// returns as soon as the launch is enqueued. Use stream or event
103	/// synchronization to wait for completion.
104	///
105	/// `grid_dim` and `block_dim` are `(x, y, z)` tuples specifying the grid and
106	/// block dimensions respectively. `shared_mem_bytes` reserves per-block dynamic
107	/// shared memory (in bytes).
108	///
109	/// `kernel_params` is a mutable slice of pointers, one per kernel argument,
110	/// each pointing to the argument value. The `extra` parameter is set to null
111	/// (use raw [`sys::cuLaunchKernel`] directly if you need more advanced launch
112	/// modes).
113	///
114	/// This helper performs **no context binding**. Prefer
115	/// [`launch_kernel_on_stream`] for normal host-side code: it takes a typed
116	/// [`CudaStream`], binds that stream's owning context to the calling thread,
117	/// and then forwards to this raw entry point.
118	///
119	/// # Safety
120	///
121	/// - `func` must be a valid `CUfunction` obtained from a loaded module.
122	/// - `stream` must be a valid `CUstream` belonging to the current context, or
123	///   null for the default stream.
124	/// - Each element of `kernel_params` must point to a region of memory that
125	///   matches the corresponding kernel parameter in size and alignment.
126	/// - The pointed-to argument values must remain valid until this function
127	///   returns, because the driver reads them during launch submission.
128	/// - The grid and block dimensions must not exceed device limits.
129	/// - The calling thread must already have the context that owns both `func` and
130	///   `stream` bound as its current context.
131	///
132	/// # Errors
133	///
134	/// Returns the CUDA driver error produced by `cuLaunchKernel` if launch
135	/// submission fails.
136	#[inline]
137	pub unsafe fn launch_kernel(
138	    func: cuda_bindings::CUfunction,
139	    grid_dim: (u32, u32, u32),
140	    block_dim: (u32, u32, u32),
141	    shared_mem_bytes: u32,
142	    stream: cuda_bindings::CUstream,
143	    kernel_params: &mut [*mut std::ffi::c_void],
144	) -> Result<(), DriverError> {
145	    unsafe {
146	        cuda_bindings::cuLaunchKernel(
147	            func,
148	            grid_dim.0,
149	            grid_dim.1,
150	            grid_dim.2,
151	            block_dim.0,
152	            block_dim.1,
153	            block_dim.2,
154	            shared_mem_bytes,
155	            stream,
156	            kernel_params.as_mut_ptr(),
157	            std::ptr::null_mut(),
158	        )
159	    }
160	    .result()
161	}

163	/// Launches a CUDA kernel on a specific [`CudaStream`], binding its context first.
164	///
165	/// This is the usual host-side helper for `cuda_launch!` and async launches.
166	/// It ensures the stream's owning context is current before calling the raw
167	/// [`launch_kernel`] entry point, so callers do not need to manually call
168	/// [`CudaContext::bind_to_thread`] before every launch.
169	///
170	/// Unlike [`launch_kernel`], this helper works with typed wrappers rather than
171	/// raw driver handles. It is therefore the preferred API whenever you already
172	/// have a [`CudaFunction`] and [`CudaStream`].
173	///
174	/// # Safety
175	///
176	/// - `func` must refer to a kernel loaded from the same CUDA context that owns
177	///   `stream`.
178	/// - Each element of `kernel_params` must point to a region of memory that
179	///   matches the corresponding kernel parameter in size and alignment.
180	/// - The pointed-to argument values must remain valid until this function
181	///   returns.
182	/// - The grid and block dimensions must not exceed device limits.
183	///
184	/// # Errors
185	///
186	/// Returns an error if binding `stream.context()` fails or if the underlying
187	/// `cuLaunchKernel` call rejects the launch.
188	#[inline]
189	pub unsafe fn launch_kernel_on_stream(
190	    func: &CudaFunction,
191	    grid_dim: (u32, u32, u32),
192	    block_dim: (u32, u32, u32),
193	    shared_mem_bytes: u32,
194	    stream: &CudaStream,
195	    kernel_params: &mut [*mut std::ffi::c_void],
196	) -> Result<(), DriverError> {
197	    stream.context().bind_to_thread()?;
198	    unsafe {
199	        launch_kernel(
200	            func.cu_function(),
201	            grid_dim,
202	            block_dim,
203	            shared_mem_bytes,
204	            stream.cu_stream(),
205	            kernel_params,
206	        )
207	    }
208	}

210	/// Low-level wrapper around `cuLaunchKernelEx`.
211	///
212	/// This is the cluster-aware launch path. It builds a `CUlaunchConfig` with a
213	/// `CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_CLUSTER_DIMENSION` attribute set to `cluster_dim`.
214	/// Required for thread-block cluster launches on sm_90+ (Hopper / Blackwell).
215	///
216	/// This helper performs **no context binding**. Prefer
217	/// [`launch_kernel_ex_on_stream`] in normal host-side code so the correct
218	/// stream context is made current automatically.
219	///
220	/// # Safety
221	///
222	/// Same preconditions as [`launch_kernel`], plus:
223	/// - Each component of `cluster_dim` must divide the corresponding `grid_dim`
224	///   component.
225	/// - The total cluster size must not exceed the device maximum.
226	/// - The device must support compute capability 9.0 or higher.
227	///
228	/// # Errors
229	///
230	/// Returns the CUDA driver error produced by `cuLaunchKernelEx` if launch
231	/// submission fails.
232	#[inline]
233	pub unsafe fn launch_kernel_ex(
234	    func: cuda_bindings::CUfunction,
235	    grid_dim: (u32, u32, u32),
236	    block_dim: (u32, u32, u32),
237	    shared_mem_bytes: u32,
238	    cluster_dim: (u32, u32, u32),
239	    stream: cuda_bindings::CUstream,
240	    kernel_params: &mut [*mut std::ffi::c_void],
241	) -> Result<(), DriverError> {
242	    // CUlaunchAttribute_st is opaque (see cuda-bindings/build.rs) for CUDA 13.2+
243	    // compatibility. C layout: { id: u32 @ 0, pad: [u8;4] @ 4, value: union @ 8 }.
244	    // clusterDim is three u32 fields (x, y, z) at offset 0 within the value union.
245	    let mut cluster_attr: cuda_bindings::CUlaunchAttribute_st = unsafe { std::mem::zeroed() };
246	    unsafe {
247	        let base = &mut cluster_attr as *mut _ as *mut u8;
248	        // id at offset 0
249	        (base as *mut u32)
250	            .write(cuda_bindings::CUlaunchAttributeID_enum_CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_CLUSTER_DIMENSION);
251	        // clusterDim.x/y/z at offsets 8, 12, 16
252	        let dim_ptr = base.add(8) as *mut u32;
253	        dim_ptr.write(cluster_dim.0);
254	        dim_ptr.add(1).write(cluster_dim.1);
255	        dim_ptr.add(2).write(cluster_dim.2);
256	    }
257	
258	    let config = cuda_bindings::CUlaunchConfig_st {
259	        gridDimX: grid_dim.0,
260	        gridDimY: grid_dim.1,
261	        gridDimZ: grid_dim.2,
262	        blockDimX: block_dim.0,
263	        blockDimY: block_dim.1,
264	        blockDimZ: block_dim.2,
265	        sharedMemBytes: shared_mem_bytes,
266	        hStream: stream,
267	        attrs: &mut cluster_attr,
268	        numAttrs: 1,
269	    };
270	
271	    unsafe {
272	        cuda_bindings::cuLaunchKernelEx(
273	            &config,
274	            func,
275	            kernel_params.as_mut_ptr(),
276	            std::ptr::null_mut(),
277	        )
278	    }
279	    .result()
280	}

328	/// Low-level wrapper around `cuLaunchKernelEx` with the
329	/// `CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_COOPERATIVE` flag set.
330	///
331	/// A *cooperative* launch guarantees that every block in the grid is
332	/// co-resident on the device, which is the precondition for grid-wide
333	/// barriers like `cuda_device::grid::sync()`. The CUDA driver also
334	/// populates PTX environment registers `%envreg1` / `%envreg2` with the
335	/// pointer to the per-launch grid workspace; the device-side barrier
336	/// implementation reads those registers to find the shared counter.
337	///
338	/// This helper performs **no context binding**. Prefer
339	/// [`launch_kernel_cooperative_on_stream`] in normal host-side code so the
340	/// correct stream context is made current automatically.
341	///
342	/// # Safety
343	///
344	/// Same preconditions as [`launch_kernel`], plus:
345	/// - The device must support cooperative launch (`CU_DEVICE_ATTRIBUTE_COOPERATIVE_LAUNCH`).
346	/// - The grid must fit in the maximum number of resident blocks for this
347	///   kernel as reported by `cuOccupancyMaxActiveBlocksPerMultiprocessor`
348	///   (otherwise `cuLaunchKernelEx` returns
349	///   `CUDA_ERROR_COOPERATIVE_LAUNCH_TOO_LARGE`).
350	///
351	/// # Errors
352	///
353	/// Returns the CUDA driver error produced by `cuLaunchKernelEx` if launch
354	/// submission fails.
355	#[inline]
356	pub unsafe fn launch_kernel_cooperative(
357	    func: cuda_bindings::CUfunction,
358	    grid_dim: (u32, u32, u32),
359	    block_dim: (u32, u32, u32),
360	    shared_mem_bytes: u32,
361	    stream: cuda_bindings::CUstream,
362	    kernel_params: &mut [*mut std::ffi::c_void],
363	) -> Result<(), DriverError> {
364	    // CUlaunchAttribute_st is opaque (see cuda-bindings/build.rs) for CUDA 13.2+
365	    // compatibility. C layout: { id: u32 @ 0, pad: [u8;4] @ 4, value: union @ 8 }.
366	    // For the COOPERATIVE attribute the value union holds a single `int cooperative`
367	    // at offset 0 — set to 1 to enable, 0 to disable.
368	    let mut coop_attr: cuda_bindings::CUlaunchAttribute_st = unsafe { std::mem::zeroed() };
369	    unsafe {
370	        let base = &mut coop_attr as *mut _ as *mut u8;
371	        (base as *mut u32)
372	            .write(cuda_bindings::CUlaunchAttributeID_enum_CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_COOPERATIVE);
373	        let val_ptr = base.add(8) as *mut i32;
374	        val_ptr.write(1);
375	    }
376	
377	    let config = cuda_bindings::CUlaunchConfig_st {
378	        gridDimX: grid_dim.0,
379	        gridDimY: grid_dim.1,
380	        gridDimZ: grid_dim.2,
381	        blockDimX: block_dim.0,
382	        blockDimY: block_dim.1,
383	        blockDimZ: block_dim.2,
384	        sharedMemBytes: shared_mem_bytes,
385	        hStream: stream,
386	        attrs: &mut coop_attr,
387	        numAttrs: 1,
388	    };
389	
390	    unsafe {
391	        cuda_bindings::cuLaunchKernelEx(
392	            &config,
393	            func,
394	            kernel_params.as_mut_ptr(),
395	            std::ptr::null_mut(),
396	        )
397	    }
398	    .result()
399	}

438	/// Low-level wrapper around `cuLaunchKernelEx` with **both** the
439	/// `CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_CLUSTER_DIMENSION` and
440	/// `CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_COOPERATIVE` attributes set.
441	///
442	/// `cuLaunchKernelEx` takes an array of launch attributes, so a single call
443	/// can request thread-block clusters ([`launch_kernel_ex`]) and a
444	/// cooperative launch ([`launch_kernel_cooperative`]) at the same time.
445	/// This is the path used when a `#[cuda_module]` kernel carries both
446	/// `#[cluster_launch(...)]` and `#[cooperative_launch]`.
447	///
448	/// This helper performs **no context binding**. Prefer
449	/// [`launch_kernel_ex_cooperative_on_stream`] in normal host-side code so
450	/// the correct stream context is made current automatically.
451	///
452	/// # Safety
453	///
454	/// The combined preconditions of [`launch_kernel_ex`] (cluster dimensions
455	/// must divide the grid, sm_90+) and [`launch_kernel_cooperative`] (the
456	/// device must support cooperative launch and the whole grid must be
457	/// co-resident).
458	///
459	/// # Errors
460	///
461	/// Returns the CUDA driver error produced by `cuLaunchKernelEx` if launch
462	/// submission fails.
463	#[inline]
464	pub unsafe fn launch_kernel_ex_cooperative(
465	    func: cuda_bindings::CUfunction,
466	    grid_dim: (u32, u32, u32),
467	    block_dim: (u32, u32, u32),
468	    shared_mem_bytes: u32,
469	    cluster_dim: (u32, u32, u32),
470	    stream: cuda_bindings::CUstream,
471	    kernel_params: &mut [*mut std::ffi::c_void],
472	) -> Result<(), DriverError> {
473	    // CUlaunchAttribute_st is opaque (see cuda-bindings/build.rs) for CUDA 13.2+
474	    // compatibility. C layout: { id: u32 @ 0, pad: [u8;4] @ 4, value: union @ 8 }.
475	    // attrs[0]: clusterDim — three u32 fields (x, y, z) at offset 0 of the union.
476	    // attrs[1]: cooperative — a single `int` at offset 0 of the union; 1 = enabled.
477	    let mut attrs: [cuda_bindings::CUlaunchAttribute_st; 2] = unsafe { std::mem::zeroed() };
478	    unsafe {
479	        let base = &mut attrs[0] as *mut _ as *mut u8;
480	        (base as *mut u32)
481	            .write(cuda_bindings::CUlaunchAttributeID_enum_CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_CLUSTER_DIMENSION);
482	        let dim_ptr = base.add(8) as *mut u32;
483	        dim_ptr.write(cluster_dim.0);
484	        dim_ptr.add(1).write(cluster_dim.1);
485	        dim_ptr.add(2).write(cluster_dim.2);
486	
487	        let base = &mut attrs[1] as *mut _ as *mut u8;
488	        (base as *mut u32)
489	            .write(cuda_bindings::CUlaunchAttributeID_enum_CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_COOPERATIVE);
490	        let val_ptr = base.add(8) as *mut i32;
491	        val_ptr.write(1);
492	    }
493	
494	    let config = cuda_bindings::CUlaunchConfig_st {
495	        gridDimX: grid_dim.0,
496	        gridDimY: grid_dim.1,
497	        gridDimZ: grid_dim.2,
498	        blockDimX: block_dim.0,
499	        blockDimY: block_dim.1,
500	        blockDimZ: block_dim.2,
501	        sharedMemBytes: shared_mem_bytes,
502	        hStream: stream,
503	        attrs: attrs.as_mut_ptr(),
504	        numAttrs: 2,
505	    };
506	
507	    unsafe {
508	        cuda_bindings::cuLaunchKernelEx(
509	            &config,
510	            func,
511	            kernel_params.as_mut_ptr(),
512	            std::ptr::null_mut(),
513	        )
514	    }
515	    .result()
516	}

534	pub unsafe fn launch_kernel_ex_cooperative_on_stream(

539	    cluster_dim: (u32, u32, u32),
540	    stream: &CudaStream,
541	    kernel_params: &mut [*mut std::ffi::c_void],
542	) -> Result<(), DriverError> {
543	    stream.context().bind_to_thread()?;
544	    unsafe {
545	        launch_kernel_ex_cooperative(
546	            func.cu_function(),
547	            grid_dim,
548	            block_dim,
549	            shared_mem_bytes,
550	            cluster_dim,
551	            stream.cu_stream(),
552	            kernel_params,
553	        )
554	    }
555	}

6	//! Owning device memory buffer with ergonomic host-device transfer methods.
7	//!
8	//! [`DeviceBuffer<T>`] is analogous to `Vec<T>` on the host: it owns a
9	//! contiguous allocation of `len` elements on the device and frees it on
10	//! drop. Unlike cudarc's `CudaSlice`, the buffer carries no stream reference
11	//! and no hidden event tracking -- the stream is an explicit parameter on
12	//! every transfer operation, making data-flow and synchronization transparent.
13	//!
14	//! # Quick start
15	//!
16	//! ```ignore
17	//! let a_dev = DeviceBuffer::from_host(&stream, &a_host)?;
18	//! let c_dev = DeviceBuffer::<f32>::zeroed(&stream, N)?;
19	//! // ... kernel launch ...
20	//! let c_host = c_dev.to_host_vec(&stream)?;
21	//! ```
22

35	/// Marker trait for values that can be safely copied between host and device
36	/// memory as raw bytes.
37	///
38	/// Types implementing `DeviceCopy` must not contain Rust-owned allocations,
39	/// references, or other values whose validity depends on host-side ownership or
40	/// drop semantics. This is the device-memory equivalent of a plain-old-data
41	/// contract.
42	///
43	/// # Safety
44	///
45	/// Implementors must be safe to duplicate with a byte-for-byte copy. Values
46	/// copied back from device memory must have a bit pattern that is valid for
47	/// `Self`, and the all-zero bit pattern must also be valid because
48	/// [`DeviceBuffer::zeroed`] initializes memory with zero bytes.
49	///
50	/// `Copy` alone is not enough: types such as `bool`, `char`, and
51	/// `NonZeroU32` are `Copy`, but not every byte pattern is a valid value of
52	/// those types. `DeviceCopy` is the stronger promise required when
53	/// `DeviceBuffer` turns raw device bytes back into initialized Rust values.
54	pub unsafe trait DeviceCopy: Copy {}

114	/// Owning handle to a contiguous device allocation of `T` elements.
115	///
116	/// Holds a raw device pointer, element count, and a reference-counted
117	/// context that keeps the CUDA context alive. Dropping the buffer calls
118	/// `cuMemFree` (synchronous); for async-sensitive workloads, use
119	/// `cuda_async::DeviceBox` which frees via a deallocator stream.
120	///
121	/// Device buffers may only transfer plain device-copyable values. Owning host
122	/// types such as [`String`] are rejected because copying their bytes to and
123	/// from device memory would not preserve Rust ownership invariants.
124	///
125	/// ```compile_fail
126	/// # use cuda_core::{CudaStream, DeviceBuffer};
127	/// # fn rejects_non_device_copy(stream: &CudaStream) {
128	/// let _ = DeviceBuffer::<String>::zeroed(stream, 1);
129	/// # }
130	/// ```
131	pub struct DeviceBuffer<T> {
132	    ptr: CUdeviceptr,
133	    len: usize,
134	    num_bytes: usize,
135	    ctx: Arc<CudaContext>,
136	    /// When the allocation came from the stream-ordered pool
137	    /// (`cuMemAllocAsync`), this holds an `Arc` to the owning stream so the
138	    /// implicit `Drop` can free it with `cuMemFreeAsync` on that same stream
139	    /// (stream-ordered, race-free). `None` for synchronous (`cuMemAlloc`)
140	    /// allocations, which `Drop` frees with the synchronous `cuMemFree`.
141	    /// Freeing an async-pool pointer with the synchronous `cuMemFree` while
142	    /// stream work is still pending is a use-after-free (compute-sanitizer:
143	    /// "free-before-alloc").
144	    dealloc_stream: Option<Arc<CudaStream>>,
145	    _marker: PhantomData<T>,
146	}
147	
148	// SAFETY: CUdeviceptr is a u64 handle valid across threads when the owning
149	// context is bound. The PhantomData<T> is Send if T is Send.
150	unsafe impl<T: Send> Send for DeviceBuffer<T> {}
151	// SAFETY: &DeviceBuffer only exposes cu_deviceptr() and len(), both of which
152	// return Copy values. No interior mutability.
153	unsafe impl<T: Send + Sync> Sync for DeviceBuffer<T> {}
154	
155	impl<T> Drop for DeviceBuffer<T> {
156	    fn drop(&mut self) {
157	        if self.ptr != 0 {
158	            self.ctx.record_err(self.ctx.bind_to_thread());
159	            // Free with the allocator that matches how the memory was
160	            // allocated. Stream-ordered (`cuMemAllocAsync`) memory must be
161	            // released stream-ordered with `cuMemFreeAsync` on its owning
162	            // stream; using the synchronous `cuMemFree` here races with
163	            // pending stream work (use-after-free). Synchronous allocations
164	            // free synchronously as before.
165	            let result = match &self.dealloc_stream {
166	                Some(stream) => unsafe { crate::memory::free_async(self.ptr, stream.cu_stream()) },
167	                None => unsafe { crate::memory::free_sync(self.ptr) },
168	            };
169	            self.ctx.record_err(result);
170	        }
171	    }
172	}

174	impl<T> DeviceBuffer<T> {
175	    /// Returns the raw `CUdeviceptr` for use in kernel argument lists.
176	    #[inline]
177	    pub fn cu_deviceptr(&self) -> CUdeviceptr {
178	        self.ptr
179	    }
180	
181	    /// Number of `T` elements in the buffer.
182	    #[inline]
183	    pub fn len(&self) -> usize {
184	        self.len
185	    }
186	
187	    /// Returns `true` if the buffer has zero elements.
188	    #[inline]
189	    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {
190	        self.len == 0
191	    }
192	
193	    /// Total size in bytes (`len * size_of::<T>()`).
194	    #[inline]
195	    pub fn num_bytes(&self) -> usize {
196	        self.num_bytes
197	    }
198	
199	    /// Returns a reference to the owning context.
200	    #[inline]
201	    pub fn context(&self) -> &Arc<CudaContext> {
202	        &self.ctx
203	    }
204	
205	    /// Constructs a `DeviceBuffer` from pre-existing raw parts.
206	    ///
207	    /// # Safety
208	    ///
209	    /// - `ptr` must have been allocated via `cuMemAlloc*` with at least
210	    ///   `len * size_of::<T>()` bytes.
211	    /// - `ptr` must belong to the same CUDA context as `ctx`.
212	    /// - The caller transfers ownership -- `ptr` will be freed on drop.
213	    /// - `ptr` is assumed to be a synchronous (`cuMemAlloc`) allocation and is
214	    ///   freed with the synchronous `cuMemFree` on drop. Do not pass a
215	    ///   stream-ordered (`cuMemAllocAsync`) pointer here.
216	    ///
217	    /// # Panics
218	    ///
219	    /// Panics if `len * size_of::<T>()` overflows `usize`.
220	    pub unsafe fn from_raw_parts(ptr: CUdeviceptr, len: usize, ctx: Arc<CudaContext>) -> Self {
221	        // SAFETY: `from_raw_parts` has the same raw-allocation safety contract,
222	        // with no stream-ordered deallocation metadata attached.
223	        unsafe { Self::from_raw_parts_with_dealloc_stream(ptr, len, ctx, None) }
224	    }
225	
226	    unsafe fn from_raw_parts_with_dealloc_stream(
227	        ptr: CUdeviceptr,
228	        len: usize,
229	        ctx: Arc<CudaContext>,
230	        dealloc_stream: Option<Arc<CudaStream>>,
231	    ) -> Self {
232	        let num_bytes =
233	            allocation_size::<T>(len).expect("DeviceBuffer::from_raw_parts byte size overflow");
234	        Self {
235	            ptr,
236	            len,
237	            num_bytes,
238	            ctx,
239	            dealloc_stream,
240	            _marker: PhantomData,
241	        }
242	    }
243	
244	    /// Consumes the buffer and returns the raw parts without freeing.
245	    ///
246	    /// The caller is responsible for eventually freeing `ptr` with the
247	    /// allocator that matches how it was created. For stream-ordered
248	    /// allocations, this does not return the stored deallocation stream; the
249	    /// caller must already know which stream to use for `cuMemFreeAsync`.
250	    pub fn into_raw_parts(self) -> (CUdeviceptr, usize, Arc<CudaContext>) {
251	        let (ptr, len, ctx, _dealloc_stream) = self.into_all_raw_parts();
252	        (ptr, len, ctx)
253	    }
254	
255	    fn into_all_raw_parts(
256	        self,
257	    ) -> (
258	        CUdeviceptr,
259	        usize,
260	        Arc<CudaContext>,
261	        Option<Arc<CudaStream>>,
262	    ) {
263	        // Suppress the buffer's `Drop` (which would free `ptr`) while still
264	        // moving out the heap-owned fields. Callers that do not need
265	        // `dealloc_stream` can drop it after this helper returns.
266	        let this = std::mem::ManuallyDrop::new(self);
267	        let ptr = this.ptr;
268	        let len = this.len;
269	        // SAFETY: `this` is `ManuallyDrop` and is never used again, so reading
270	        // out its non-`Copy` fields takes ownership without a double drop.
271	        let ctx = unsafe { std::ptr::read(&this.ctx) };
272	        let dealloc_stream = unsafe { std::ptr::read(&this.dealloc_stream) };
273	        (ptr, len, ctx, dealloc_stream)
274	    }
275	
276	    /// Reinterpret the element type of this buffer as `A`.
277	    ///
278	    /// `A` must have the same size and alignment as `T` (e.g. `A` is
279	    /// `#[repr(transparent)]` over `T`). This is the "atomic-slice launch
280	    /// mapping" for issue #151: allocate and initialize a plain
281	    /// `DeviceBuffer<u64>`, then hand it to a kernel that takes
282	    /// `&[DeviceAtomicU64]`. The pointer, length, and bytes are unchanged;
283	    /// only the element type the kernel sees changes to one whose pointee
284	    /// permits shared mutation (so rustc does not mark it `readonly`/`noalias`).
285	    ///
286	    /// Element counts are preserved because `size_of::<A>() == size_of::<T>()`.
287	    pub fn cast_elem<A>(self) -> DeviceBuffer<A> {
288	        assert_eq!(
289	            std::mem::size_of::<A>(),
290	            std::mem::size_of::<T>(),
291	            "cast_elem requires the same element size"
292	        );
293	        assert_eq!(
294	            std::mem::align_of::<A>(),
295	            std::mem::align_of::<T>(),
296	            "cast_elem requires the same element alignment"
297	        );
298	        let (ptr, len, ctx, dealloc_stream) = self.into_all_raw_parts();
299	        // SAFETY: `ptr` came from a valid `DeviceBuffer<T>` allocation of `len`
300	        // elements; `A` has identical size and alignment, so the same allocation
301	        // is a valid `DeviceBuffer<A>` of the same length and the same byte
302	        // extent. Ownership transfers; the original buffer's `Drop` is
303	        // suppressed by `into_all_raw_parts`, and the allocation metadata is
304	        // preserved for the new element type.
305	        unsafe {
306	            DeviceBuffer::<A>::from_raw_parts_with_dealloc_stream(ptr, len, ctx, dealloc_stream)
307	        }
308	    }
309	}

311	impl<T: DeviceCopy> DeviceBuffer<T> {
312	    /// Allocates device memory, copies `data` from the host on `stream`, and
313	    /// synchronizes `stream` before returning.
314	    ///
315	    /// The synchronization keeps this safe for borrowed host slices: `data`
316	    /// may be dropped, reused, or mutated immediately after this function
317	    /// returns. For true host-device overlap with caller-managed source
318	    /// lifetimes, use [`Self::from_host_async_unchecked`].
319	    ///
320	    /// An empty `data` slice yields an empty buffer without touching the
321	    /// driver allocator.
322	    ///
323	    /// # Allocation safety on error
324	    ///
325	    /// The buffer takes ownership of the device allocation immediately after
326	    /// `malloc_sync`, before the fallible `memcpy_htod_async` enqueue and
327	    /// stream synchronization run. If either step fails, the early return
328	    /// drops the buffer and its `Drop` impl frees the allocation, so no
329	    /// device memory is leaked.
330	    pub fn from_host(stream: &CudaStream, data: &[T]) -> Result<Self, DriverError> {
331	        let ctx = stream.context().clone();
332	        let len = data.len();
333	        let num_bytes = allocation_size::<T>(len)?;
334	
335	        // cuMemAlloc rejects zero-byte requests with CUDA_ERROR_INVALID_VALUE,
336	        // so represent an empty buffer as a null pointer (Drop skips it).
337	        if num_bytes == 0 {
338	            // SAFETY: a null pointer with zero bytes is never dereferenced
339	            // and Drop ignores it.
340	            return Ok(unsafe { Self::from_raw_parts(0, len, ctx) });
341	        }
342	
343	        let ptr = unsafe { crate::memory::malloc_sync(num_bytes)? };
344	        // SAFETY: `ptr` was just allocated with `num_bytes` bytes in the
345	        // stream's context; ownership transfers to `buf` here so any early
346	        // return below frees it through the buffer's own `Drop`.
347	        let buf = unsafe { Self::from_raw_parts(ptr, len, ctx) };
348	        let enqueue_result = unsafe {
349	            crate::memory::memcpy_htod_async(buf.ptr, data.as_ptr(), num_bytes, stream.cu_stream())
350	        };
351	        let sync_result = stream.synchronize();
352	        enqueue_result?;
353	        sync_result?;
354	        Ok(buf)
355	    }
356	
357	    /// Allocates device memory and enqueues a host-to-device copy from `data`
358	    /// on `stream`, returning without synchronizing.
359	    ///
360	    /// # Safety
361	    ///
362	    /// This call only enqueues the host-to-device copy and returns; CUDA may
363	    /// still be reading from `data` after the borrow is released. The caller
364	    /// must ensure `data` is not dropped, freed, mutated, or aliased until the
365	    /// enqueued copy has completed, typically after the next
366	    /// [`CudaStream::synchronize`] call or a stream-ordered event wait.
367	    pub unsafe fn from_host_async_unchecked(
368	        stream: &CudaStream,
369	        data: &[T],
370	    ) -> Result<Self, DriverError> {
371	        let ctx = stream.context().clone();
372	        let len = data.len();
373	        let num_bytes = std::mem::size_of_val(data);
374	
375	        // cuMemAlloc rejects zero-byte requests with CUDA_ERROR_INVALID_VALUE,
376	        // so represent an empty buffer as a null pointer (Drop skips it).
377	        if num_bytes == 0 {
378	            // SAFETY: a null pointer with zero bytes is never dereferenced
379	            // and Drop ignores it.
380	            return Ok(unsafe { Self::from_raw_parts(0, len, ctx) });
381	        }
382	
383	        let ptr = unsafe { crate::memory::malloc_sync(num_bytes)? };
384	        // SAFETY: `ptr` was just allocated with `num_bytes` bytes in the
385	        // stream's context; ownership transfers to `buf` here so any early
386	        // return below frees it through the buffer's own `Drop`.
387	        let buf = unsafe { Self::from_raw_parts(ptr, len, ctx) };
388	        unsafe {
389	            crate::memory::memcpy_htod_async(
390	                buf.ptr,
391	                data.as_ptr(),
392	                num_bytes,
393	                stream.cu_stream(),
394	            )?;
395	        }
396	        Ok(buf)
397	    }
398	
399	    /// Allocates device memory and enqueues a host-to-device copy from a
400	    /// pinned host buffer on `stream`, returning without synchronizing.
401	    ///
402	    /// Pinned host memory allows CUDA to avoid the pageable-memory staging
403	    /// path and is required when host-device copies need true asynchronous
404	    /// overlap with other stream work.
405	    ///
406	    /// `PinnedHostBuffer` currently uses `cuMemAllocHost` without the
407	    /// `PORTABLE` flag, so the allocation is only pinned in the context that
408	    /// created it. In debug builds this asserts that `data` and `stream`
409	    /// share the same [`CudaContext`].
410	    ///
411	    /// The device-to-host counterparts are [`Self::copy_to_pinned_host`]
412	    /// (blocking) and [`Self::copy_to_pinned_host_async`] (non-blocking). To
413	    /// refill an existing device buffer instead of allocating a new one, use
414	    /// [`Self::copy_from_pinned_host_async`].
415	    ///
416	    /// # Safety
417	    ///
418	    /// This call only enqueues the host-to-device copy on `stream` and
419	    /// returns; CUDA may still be reading from `data`'s pinned pointer long
420	    /// after this function returns. The caller is responsible for ensuring
421	    /// `data` is not dropped, freed, mutated, or aliased until the enqueued
422	    /// copy has completed, typically after the next
423	    /// [`CudaStream::synchronize`] call or a stream-ordered event wait.
424	    /// Dropping `data` before that synchronization point calls
425	    /// `cuMemFreeHost` while the in-flight transfer is still reading the
426	    /// buffer, which is undefined behavior.
427	    pub unsafe fn from_pinned_host(
428	        stream: &CudaStream,
429	        data: &PinnedHostBuffer<T>,
430	    ) -> Result<Self, DriverError> {
431	        debug_assert!(
432	            Arc::ptr_eq(data.context(), stream.context()),
433	            "pinned host buffer and stream must belong to the same CUDA context"
434	        );
435	        // SAFETY: this method's safety contract requires the caller to keep
436	        // the pinned source valid until the enqueued copy completes.
437	        unsafe { Self::from_host_async_unchecked(stream, data.as_slice()) }
438	    }
439	
440	    /// Allocates zero-initialized device memory of `len` elements, enqueued
441	    /// on `stream`.
442	    ///
443	    /// A `len` of zero (or a zero-sized `T`) yields an empty buffer without
444	    /// touching the driver allocator.
445	    ///
446	    /// # Allocation safety on error
447	    ///
448	    /// The returned buffer takes ownership of the device allocation
449	    /// immediately after `malloc_sync`, before the fallible
450	    /// `memset_d8_async` enqueue runs. If the enqueue fails, the early
451	    /// return drops the buffer and its `Drop` impl frees the allocation, so
452	    /// no device memory is leaked.
453	    pub fn zeroed(stream: &CudaStream, len: usize) -> Result<Self, DriverError> {
454	        let ctx = stream.context().clone();
455	        let num_bytes = allocation_size::<T>(len)?;
456	
457	        // cuMemAlloc rejects zero-byte requests with CUDA_ERROR_INVALID_VALUE,
458	        // so represent an empty buffer as a null pointer (Drop skips it).
459	        if num_bytes == 0 {
460	            // SAFETY: a null pointer with zero bytes is never dereferenced
461	            // and Drop ignores it.
462	            return Ok(unsafe { Self::from_raw_parts(0, len, ctx) });
463	        }
464	
465	        let ptr = unsafe { crate::memory::malloc_sync(num_bytes)? };
466	        // SAFETY: `ptr` was just allocated with `num_bytes` bytes in the
467	        // stream's context; ownership transfers to `buf` here so any early
468	        // return below frees it through the buffer's own `Drop`.
469	        let buf = unsafe { Self::from_raw_parts(ptr, len, ctx) };
470	        unsafe {
471	            crate::memory::memset_d8_async(buf.ptr, 0, num_bytes, stream.cu_stream())?;
472	        }
473	        Ok(buf)
474	    }
475	
476	    /// Copies the entire buffer back to the host, returning a `Vec<T>`.
477	    ///
478	    /// Synchronizes on `stream` before returning so the host vector is safe
479	    /// to read immediately.
480	    pub fn to_host_vec(&self, stream: &CudaStream) -> Result<Vec<T>, DriverError> {
481	        let mut host = Vec::with_capacity(self.len);
482	        unsafe {
483	            crate::memory::memcpy_dtoh_async(
484	                host.as_mut_ptr(),
485	                self.ptr,
486	                self.num_bytes(),
487	                stream.cu_stream(),
488	            )?;
489	        }
490	        stream.synchronize()?;
491	        unsafe { host.set_len(self.len) };
492	        Ok(host)
493	    }
494	
495	    /// Copies the buffer contents into an existing host slice.
496	    ///
497	    /// Synchronizes on `stream` before returning. Panics if
498	    /// `dst.len() < self.len()`.
499	    pub fn copy_to_host(&self, stream: &CudaStream, dst: &mut [T]) -> Result<(), DriverError> {
500	        assert!(
501	            dst.len() >= self.len,
502	            "destination slice too small: {} < {}",
503	            dst.len(),
504	            self.len
505	        );
506	        unsafe {
507	            crate::memory::memcpy_dtoh_async(
508	                dst.as_mut_ptr(),
509	                self.ptr,
510	                self.num_bytes(),
511	                stream.cu_stream(),
512	            )?;
513	        }
514	        stream.synchronize()
515	    }
516	
517	    /// Copies the buffer contents into an existing pinned host buffer and
518	    /// synchronizes `stream` before returning.
519	    ///
520	    /// Panics if `dst.len() < self.len()`. Use pinned destinations when you
521	    /// need the transfer to avoid pageable-memory staging; this helper still
522	    /// waits for completion before returning, matching [`Self::copy_to_host`].
523	    ///
524	    /// For true DtoH overlap, use [`Self::copy_to_pinned_host_async`] and
525	    /// synchronize the stream later.
526	    pub fn copy_to_pinned_host(
527	        &self,
528	        stream: &CudaStream,
529	        dst: &mut PinnedHostBuffer<T>,
530	    ) -> Result<(), DriverError> {
531	        // SAFETY: we synchronize the stream below before returning, so the
532	        // pinned destination is no longer being written to by CUDA when the
533	        // mutable borrow on `dst` is released to the caller.
534	        unsafe { self.copy_to_pinned_host_async(stream, dst)? };
535	        stream.synchronize()
536	    }
537	
538	    /// Enqueues a device-to-host copy into an existing pinned host buffer and
539	    /// returns without synchronizing.
540	    ///
541	    /// Panics if `dst.len() < self.len()`.
542	    ///
543	    /// `PinnedHostBuffer` currently uses `cuMemAllocHost` without the
544	    /// `PORTABLE` flag, so the allocation is only pinned in the context that
545	    /// created it. In debug builds this asserts that `dst` and `stream`
546	    /// share the same [`CudaContext`].
547	    ///
548	    /// # Safety
549	    ///
550	    /// This call only enqueues the device-to-host copy on `stream` and
551	    /// returns; CUDA may still be writing into `dst`'s pinned pointer long
552	    /// after this function returns. The caller is responsible for ensuring
553	    /// `dst` is not dropped, freed, read, or aliased until the enqueued copy
554	    /// has completed, typically after the next [`CudaStream::synchronize`]
555	    /// call or a stream-ordered event wait. Dropping `dst` before that
556	    /// synchronization point calls `cuMemFreeHost` while the in-flight
557	    /// transfer is still writing the buffer, which is undefined behavior.
558	    pub unsafe fn copy_to_pinned_host_async(
559	        &self,
560	        stream: &CudaStream,
561	        dst: &mut PinnedHostBuffer<T>,
562	    ) -> Result<(), DriverError> {
563	        debug_assert!(
564	            Arc::ptr_eq(dst.context(), stream.context()),
565	            "pinned host buffer and stream must belong to the same CUDA context"
566	        );
567	        assert!(
568	            dst.len() >= self.len,
569	            "destination pinned host buffer too small: {} < {}",
570	            dst.len(),
571	            self.len
572	        );
573	        unsafe {
574	            crate::memory::memcpy_dtoh_async(
575	                dst.as_mut_ptr(),
576	                self.ptr,
577	                self.num_bytes(),
578	                stream.cu_stream(),
579	            )
580	        }
581	    }
582	
583	    /// Enqueues a host-to-device copy from a pinned host buffer into this
584	    /// device buffer and returns without synchronizing.
585	    ///
586	    /// This is the symmetric counterpart of
587	    /// [`Self::copy_to_pinned_host_async`]: it refills an existing device
588	    /// allocation from rotating pinned host stagers instead of allocating a
589	    /// fresh device buffer per refresh, which is the typical shape for
590	    /// asynchronous overlap pipelines.
591	    ///
592	    /// Panics if `src.len() > self.len()`.
593	    ///
594	    /// `PinnedHostBuffer` currently uses `cuMemAllocHost` without the
595	    /// `PORTABLE` flag, so the allocation is only pinned in the context that
596	    /// created it. In debug builds this asserts that `src` and `stream`
597	    /// share the same [`CudaContext`].
598	    ///
599	    /// # Safety
600	    ///
601	    /// This call only enqueues the host-to-device copy on `stream` and
602	    /// returns; CUDA may still be reading from `src`'s pinned pointer long
603	    /// after this function returns. The caller is responsible for ensuring
604	    /// `src` is not dropped, freed, mutated, or aliased until the enqueued
605	    /// copy has completed, typically after the next
606	    /// [`CudaStream::synchronize`] call or a stream-ordered event wait.
607	    /// Dropping `src` before that synchronization point calls
608	    /// `cuMemFreeHost` while the in-flight transfer is still reading the
609	    /// buffer, which is undefined behavior.
610	    pub unsafe fn copy_from_pinned_host_async(
611	        &mut self,
612	        stream: &CudaStream,
613	        src: &PinnedHostBuffer<T>,
614	    ) -> Result<(), DriverError> {
615	        debug_assert!(
616	            Arc::ptr_eq(src.context(), stream.context()),
617	            "pinned host buffer and stream must belong to the same CUDA context"
618	        );
619	        assert!(
620	            src.len() <= self.len,
621	            "source pinned host buffer too large: {} > {}",
622	            src.len(),
623	            self.len
624	        );
625	        let num_bytes = src.num_bytes();
626	        unsafe {
627	            crate::memory::memcpy_htod_async(self.ptr, src.as_ptr(), num_bytes, stream.cu_stream())
628	        }
629	    }
630	
631	    /// Allocates `len` elements of uninitialized device memory, enqueued on
632	    /// `stream`.
633	    ///
634	    /// Unlike [`Self::zeroed`], no `cuMemsetD8` is enqueued. The contents of
635	    /// the returned buffer are undefined until the caller writes them.
636	    ///
637	    /// The buffer co-owns `stream` (via the `Arc`) so its implicit `Drop` can
638	    /// release the stream-ordered allocation with `cuMemFreeAsync` on the same
639	    /// stream. Call [`Self::drop_async`] to free explicitly on a chosen stream
640	    /// instead.
641	    ///
642	    /// # Safety
643	    ///
644	    /// Reading from the returned buffer before any kernel or memcpy has
645	    /// written it is undefined behavior.
646	    pub unsafe fn uninitialized_async(
647	        stream: &Arc<CudaStream>,
648	        len: usize,
649	    ) -> Result<Self, DriverError> {
650	        let ctx = stream.context().clone();
651	        let num_bytes = allocation_size::<T>(len)?;
652	        if num_bytes == 0 {
653	            // SAFETY: a null pointer with zero bytes is never dereferenced
654	            // and Drop/drop_async ignore it.
655	            return Ok(unsafe { Self::from_raw_parts(0, len, ctx) });
656	        }
657	
658	        let ptr = unsafe { crate::memory::malloc_async(stream.cu_stream(), num_bytes)? };
659	        Ok(Self {
660	            ptr,
661	            len,
662	            num_bytes,
663	            ctx,
664	            dealloc_stream: Some(stream.clone()),
665	            _marker: PhantomData,
666	        })
667	    }
668	
669	    /// Copies `other` into `self` device-to-device, enqueued on `stream`.
670	    ///
671	    /// Panics if `other.len() != self.len()`.
672	    pub fn copy_from_device_async(
673	        &mut self,
674	        other: &DeviceBuffer<T>,
675	        stream: &CudaStream,
676	    ) -> Result<(), DriverError> {
677	        assert_eq!(
678	            self.len, other.len,
679	            "device-to-device copy length mismatch: dst {} != src {}",
680	            self.len, other.len
681	        );
682	        if self.num_bytes() == 0 {
683	            return Ok(());
684	        }
685	        unsafe {
686	            crate::memory::memcpy_dtod_async(
687	                self.ptr,
688	                other.ptr,
689	                self.num_bytes(),
690	                stream.cu_stream(),
691	            )
692	        }
693	    }
694	
695	    /// Copies `src` into `self` host-to-device on `stream` and synchronizes
696	    /// `stream` before returning.
697	    ///
698	    /// The synchronization keeps this safe for borrowed host slices: `src`
699	    /// may be dropped, reused, or mutated immediately after this function
700	    /// returns. Panics if `src.len() != self.len()`.
701	    pub fn copy_from_host(&mut self, stream: &CudaStream, src: &[T]) -> Result<(), DriverError> {
702	        // SAFETY: this safe wrapper synchronizes `stream` before returning,
703	        // so the borrowed host slice cannot be used by CUDA after this call.
704	        let enqueue_result = unsafe { self.copy_from_host_async_unchecked(stream, src) };
705	        let sync_result = if self.num_bytes() == 0 {
706	            Ok(())
707	        } else {
708	            stream.synchronize()
709	        };
710	        enqueue_result?;
711	        sync_result
712	    }
713	
714	    /// Copies `src` into `self` host-to-device, enqueued on `stream`, and
715	    /// returns without synchronizing.
716	    ///
717	    /// # Safety
718	    ///
719	    /// This call only enqueues the host-to-device copy and returns; CUDA may
720	    /// still be reading from `src` after the borrow is released. The caller
721	    /// must ensure `src` is not dropped, freed, mutated, or aliased until the
722	    /// enqueued copy has completed, typically after the next
723	    /// [`CudaStream::synchronize`] call or a stream-ordered event wait.
724	    /// Panics if `src.len() != self.len()`.
725	    pub unsafe fn copy_from_host_async_unchecked(
726	        &mut self,
727	        stream: &CudaStream,
728	        src: &[T],
729	    ) -> Result<(), DriverError> {
730	        assert_eq!(
731	            self.len,
732	            src.len(),
733	            "host-to-device copy length mismatch: dst {} != src {}",
734	            self.len,
735	            src.len()
736	        );
737	        if self.num_bytes() == 0 {
738	            return Ok(());
739	        }
740	        unsafe {
741	            crate::memory::memcpy_htod_async(
742	                self.ptr,
743	                src.as_ptr(),
744	                self.num_bytes(),
745	                stream.cu_stream(),
746	            )
747	        }
748	    }
749	
750	    /// Consumes the buffer and frees it asynchronously on `stream`.
751	    ///
752	    /// Use this for buffers whose lifetime must be ordered relative to in-flight
753	    /// stream work.
754	    pub fn drop_async(self, stream: &CudaStream) -> Result<(), DriverError> {
755	        let (ptr, _len, _ctx) = self.into_raw_parts();
756	        if ptr == 0 {
757	            return Ok(());
758	        }
759	        unsafe { crate::memory::free_async(ptr, stream.cu_stream()) }
760	    }
761	
762	    /// Zeroes every byte in the buffer asynchronously on `stream`.
763	    pub fn zero_async(&mut self, stream: &CudaStream) -> Result<(), DriverError> {
764	        if self.num_bytes() == 0 {
765	            return Ok(());
766	        }
767	        unsafe { crate::memory::memset_d8_async(self.ptr, 0, self.num_bytes(), stream.cu_stream()) }
768	    }
769	}
770	
771	fn allocation_size<T>(len: usize) -> Result<usize, DriverError> {
772	    len.checked_mul(std::mem::size_of::<T>()).ok_or(DriverError(
773	        cuda_bindings::cudaError_enum_CUDA_ERROR_INVALID_VALUE,
774	    ))
775	}

1	/*

6	//! Async execution layer for CUDA device operations.
7	//!
8	//! This crate provides a futures-based interface for composing, scheduling, and
9	//! executing GPU work. The core abstraction is [`DeviceOperation`], a lazy,
10	//! composable representation of GPU work that is decoupled from any particular
11	//! CUDA stream or device until the moment it is scheduled.
12	//!
13	//! # Architecture
14	//!
15	//! ```text
16	//!   DeviceOperation  ──schedule()──>  DeviceFuture  ──.await──>  Result<T>
17	//!         │                                │
18	//!    (lazy graph)                    (stream assigned)
19	//!         │                                │
20	//!    combinators:                    SchedulingPolicy
21	//!    and_then, zip,                  picks the stream
22	//!    with_context, ...
23	//! ```
24	//!
25	//! ## DeviceOperation model
26	//!
27	//! A [`DeviceOperation`] is a lazy description of GPU work. It carries no stream
28	//! affinity and performs no side effects when constructed. Work is only submitted
29	//! to the GPU when the operation is *executed* inside an [`ExecutionContext`],
30	//! which binds it to a concrete CUDA stream.
31	//!
32	//! Operations compose through combinators (`and_then`, `zip`, `apply`) to build
33	//! complex dataflow graphs that remain stream-agnostic until scheduling time.
34	//!
35	//! ## Lazy scheduling
36	//!
37	//! Scheduling is the act of pairing an operation with a stream. A
38	//! [`SchedulingPolicy`] selects the stream (e.g., round-robin across a pool) and
39	//! returns a [`DeviceFuture`] that can be `.await`-ed. Until scheduled, no GPU
40	//! resources are consumed.
41	//!
42	//! ## DeviceFuture bridge
43	//!
44	//! [`DeviceFuture`] implements [`std::future::Future`]. On first poll it
45	//! executes the operation on its assigned stream, then registers a host-side
46	//! callback via `cuLaunchHostFunc`. The callback wakes the future through an
47	//! [`AtomicWaker`], yielding the result without busy-waiting.
48	//!
49	//! ## Scheduling policies
50	//!
51	//! Policies live in [`scheduling_policies`] and control stream assignment:
52	//!
53	//! | Policy                   | Behaviour                                    |
54	//! |--------------------------|----------------------------------------------|
55	//! | [`StreamPoolRoundRobin`] | Rotates across *N* streams for overlap       |
56	//! | [`SingleStream`]         | Serialises all work onto one stream          |
57	//!
58	//! [`DeviceOperation`]: device_operation::DeviceOperation
59	//! [`ExecutionContext`]: device_operation::ExecutionContext
60	//! [`DeviceFuture`]: device_future::DeviceFuture
61	//! [`SchedulingPolicy`]: scheduling_policies::SchedulingPolicy
62	//! [`StreamPoolRoundRobin`]: scheduling_policies::StreamPoolRoundRobin
63	//! [`SingleStream`]: scheduling_policies::SingleStream
64	//! [`AtomicWaker`]: futures::task::AtomicWaker
65

69	pub mod device_operation;
70	pub mod error;
71	pub mod launch;
72	pub mod reclaim;
73	pub mod scheduling_policies;
74	
75	pub use futures;

6	// Required for `type_id_u128`'s use of `core::intrinsics::type_id`. The

8	// uses while keeping the bound at `T: ?Sized` (stable `TypeId::of` would
9	// force `T: 'static` on every kernel marker — see `type_id.rs` for why
10	// that would silently reject non-`'static` borrowing closures).

13	// `rustc_codegen_cuda` against `rustc_private` and pins a nightly
14	// toolchain, so the broader project is firmly inside rustc's internal API
15	// surface. Anyone trying to lift this helper to a stable crate will hit
16	// the same gate and have to make the same trade-off there.
17	#![feature(core_intrinsics)]
18	#![allow(internal_features)]

20	//! Host-side utilities for CUDA kernel development.
21	//!
22	//! This crate provides CPU-side utilities for preparing data and setting up
23	//! GPU kernel execution. Unlike `cuda-device` which contains device-side primitives,
24	//! this crate runs entirely on the host.
25	//!
26	//! ## Modules
27	//!
28	//! - [`embedded`]: Load `#[cuda_module]` artifact bundles embedded in the host
29	//!   binary (PTX, cubin, NVVM IR, LTOIR)
30	//! - [`launch`]: Kernel launch traits (`CudaKernel`, `GenericCudaKernel`)
31	//! - [`kernel_family`]: Bounded variant menus with validated selection,
32	//!   overrides, and cache provenance
33	//! - [`ltoir`]: libNVVM + nvJitLink wrappers (`load_kernel_module`, in-memory
34	//!   cubin builders, and pre-Blackwell PTX compatibility)
35	//! - [`tiling`]: Layout transformations for tensor core operations (tcgen05)
36	//!
37	//! ## Macros
38	//!
39	//! - [`cuda_module`]: Generate a typed embedded-module loader and per-kernel
40	//!   sync launch methods from an inline kernel module. Enable the `async`
41	//!   feature for borrowed and owned async launch methods.
42	//! - [`cuda_launch!`]: Unsafe low-level launch macro. It cannot check
43	//!   argument count or types, so callers must wrap it in `unsafe { }`. Its
44	//!   niche is modules loaded at runtime by name; for embedded kernels use
45	//!   `#[cuda_module]`.
46	//! - `cuda_launch_async!`: Low-level async launch macro retained for
47	//!   migration when the `async` feature is enabled.
48	//!
49	//! ## Usage
50	//!
51	//! ```ignore
52	//! use cuda_device::{kernel, thread, DisjointSlice};
53	//! use cuda_host::cuda_module;
54	//! use cuda_core::{CudaContext, DeviceBuffer, LaunchConfig};
55	//!
56	//! #[cuda_module]
57	//! mod kernels {
58	//!     use super::*;
59	//!
60	//!     #[kernel]
61	//!     pub fn vecadd(a: &[f32], b: &[f32], mut c: DisjointSlice<f32>) { ... }
62	//! }
63	//!
64	//! let ctx = CudaContext::new(0)?;
65	//! let stream = ctx.default_stream();
66	//! let module = kernels::load(&ctx)?;
67	//!
68	//! let a_dev = DeviceBuffer::from_host(&stream, &a_host)?;
69	//! let b_dev = DeviceBuffer::from_host(&stream, &b_host)?;
70	//! let mut c_dev = DeviceBuffer::<f32>::zeroed(&stream, N)?;
71	//!
72	//! module.vecadd(
73	//!     &stream,
74	//!     LaunchConfig::for_num_elems(N as u32),
75	//!     &a_dev,
76	//!     &b_dev,
77	//!     &mut c_dev,
78	//! )?;
79	//!
80	//! let c_host = c_dev.to_host_vec(&stream)?;
81	//! ```

123	/// handles NVVM IR (`<name>.ll`) or an existing `<name>.ltoir`, and finally a
124	/// standalone cubin. NVVM inputs become a same-target cubin, except that an

1	/*
