3	 * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
4	 */
5	
6	//! Build a cubin from cuda-oxide's NVVM IR output via libNVVM + libdevice + nvJitLink.
7	//!
8	//! When a kernel uses Rust float math intrinsics (`sin`, `cos`, `exp`, `pow`,
9	//! ...), cuda-oxide lowers them to CUDA `__nv_*` libdevice calls, auto-detects
10	//! their presence, emits NVVM IR (`<name>.ll`) instead of `.ptx`, and skips
11	//! `llc`. The application then has to:
12	//!
13	//! 1. Compile the NVVM IR to LTOIR via libNVVM, with libdevice added so the
14	//!    `__nv_*` symbols are inlined.
15	//! 2. Link the resulting LTOIR via nvJitLink to produce either a cubin for
16	//!    the same architecture, or PTX when a pre-Blackwell module is loaded on
17	//!    a Blackwell GPU.
18	//! 3. Load that image with the CUDA driver.
19	//!
20	//! This module wraps that pipeline behind file and in-memory helpers:
21	//!
22	//! - [`build_cubin_from_ll`] -- explicit form, takes a `.ll` path and arch.
23	//! - [`load_kernel_module`] -- the convenience form. Looks at the example's
24	//!   directory and selects PTX, cubin, NVVM IR, or LTOIR using the recorded
25	//!   artifact mode. Use this for normal module loading.
26	//!
27	//! All work is done via [`libnvvm_sys`] and [`nvjitlink_sys`] (`dlopen` of
28	//! `libnvvm.so` and `libnvJitLink.so` from the CUDA Toolkit). No external
29	//! C tools are required, no symlinked `tools/` directory, no boilerplate.
30	//!
31	//! # Discovery
32	//!
33	//! - **libNVVM**: `LIBNVVM_PATH`, then `<root>/nvvm/lib64/libnvvm.so` for
34	//!   `<root>` in `CUDA_TOOLKIT_PATH`, `CUDA_HOME`, `CUDA_PATH`,
35	//!   `/usr/local/cuda`, `/opt/cuda`, then the system loader.
36	//! - **nvJitLink**: the same order, using `LIBNVJITLINK_PATH` and
37	//!   `<root>/lib64/libnvJitLink.so`.
38	//! - **libdevice**: `CUDA_OXIDE_LIBDEVICE` env var, then
39	//!   `<root>/nvvm/libdevice/libdevice.10.bc` for the same roots.
40	//! - **Artifact target**: the emitted `<name>.target` file, then the explicit
41	//!   `CUDA_OXIDE_TARGET` override (set by `cargo oxide --arch=<sm_XX>`).
42	//!   The CUDA context is queried separately for the GPU that will execute the
43	//!   module.
44	//!
45	//! # Native cubin cache
46	//!
47	//! File-backed NVVM IR and LTOIR use a persistent cache only when they can run
48	//! as a native cubin on the current GPU. Ordinary PTX and the pre-Blackwell to
49	//! Blackwell PTX bridge keep their normal paths.
50	//!
51	//! An entry is keyed by the exact input bytes, normalized target, module names,
52	//! ordered compiler/linker options, libdevice bytes, and SHA-256 digests of the
53	//! exact loaded `libnvvm.so` and `libnvJitLink.so` files. Unknown tool identity
54	//! disables reuse. Entries are published atomically below
55	//! `.oxide-artifacts/ltoir-cubin-cache/v1` and are rechecked before their owned
56	//! bytes are passed to the CUDA driver. The first fingerprinted compiler and
57	//! linker handles are retained for the process lifetime, so the code that runs
58	//! cannot drift away from the digest. Restart the process to select another
59	//! toolkit or pick up an in-place toolkit upgrade.
60	//!
61	//! # Example
62	//!
63	//! ```no_run
64	//! use cuda_core::CudaContext;
65	//! use cuda_host::ltoir;
66	//!
67	//! let ctx = CudaContext::new(0)?;
68	//! // Loads my_kernel.cubin (or builds + loads from my_kernel.ll).
69	//! let module = ltoir::load_kernel_module(&ctx, "my_kernel")?;
70	//! # Ok::<_, Box<dyn std::error::Error>>(())
71	//! ```
72	
73	use crate::ltoir_cache::{
74	    BuiltArtifacts, CacheKeyBuilder, CacheResult, cache_or_build, digest_file_handle,
75	};
76	use cuda_core::{CudaContext, CudaModule, DriverError};
77	use libnvvm_sys::{CudaArch, CudaArchParseError, LibNvvm, NvvmError, Program};
78	use nvjitlink_sys::{InputType, LibNvJitLink, Linker, NvJitLinkError};
79	use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
80	use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex, OnceLock};
81	use thiserror::Error;
82	
83	// Bump this whenever an unlisted compiler/linker input or pipeline semantic
84	// changes. Explicit options are also part of each key below.
85	const CACHE_RECIPE: &[u8] = b"cuda-host/native-ltoir-cubin/v1";
86	
87	struct LoadedNvvmTool {
88	    library: Arc<LibNvvm>,
89	    digest: Option<[u8; 32]>,
90	}
91	
92	struct LoadedNvJitLinkTool {
93	    library: Arc<LibNvJitLink>,
94	    digest: Option<[u8; 32]>,
95	}
96	
97	static NVVM_TOOL: OnceLock<Arc<LoadedNvvmTool>> = OnceLock::new();
98	static NVJITLINK_TOOL: OnceLock<Arc<LoadedNvJitLinkTool>> = OnceLock::new();

106	/// Failures while building or loading a module via the LTOIR pipeline.
107	#[derive(Debug, Error)]
108	pub enum LtoirError {
109	    /// A target string was not a concrete CUDA architecture.
110	    #[error(transparent)]
111	    InvalidTarget(#[from] CudaArchParseError),
112	
113	    /// NVVM IR/LTOIR was loaded without its original target architecture.
114	    #[error(
115	        "NVVM IR/LTOIR does not record its CUDA target. Keep the generated .target file, \
116	         rebuild the artifact, or explicitly set CUDA_OXIDE_TARGET to its original target."
117	    )]
118	    TargetNotFound,
119	
120	    /// The caller tried to compile target-specific NVVM IR for another target.
121	    #[error(
122	        "NVVM IR target mismatch: {ir_path} was emitted for {emitted}, but the loader requested {requested}"
123	    )]
124	    TargetMismatch {
125	        ir_path: PathBuf,
126	        emitted: String,
127	        requested: String,
128	    },
129	
130	    /// An artifact is not compatible with the context's GPU.
131	    #[error("cannot execute CUDA artifact emitted for {emitted} on {execution}: {reason}")]
132	    IncompatibleExecutionTarget {
133	        /// Architecture for which the NVVM IR or LTOIR was emitted.
134	        emitted: String,
135	        /// Numeric architecture reported by the CUDA context.
136	        execution: String,
137	        /// Why the artifact is incompatible.
138	        reason: &'static str,
139	    },
140	
141	    /// The installed toolkit does not accept the NVVM IR version cuda-oxide emits.
142	    #[error("installed libNVVM accepts NVVM IR {major}.{minor}, but cuda-oxide emits NVVM IR 2.0")]
143	    UnsupportedNvvmIrVersion { major: i32, minor: i32 },
144	
145	    /// Runtime toolkit dialect discovery disagreed with cuda-oxide's target policy.
146	    #[error(
147	        "libNVVM reports LLVM {llvm_major} for {target}, which disagrees with cuda-oxide's expected {expected} dialect"
148	    )]
149	    DialectMismatch {
150	        target: String,
151	        llvm_major: i32,
152	        expected: &'static str,
153	    },
154	
155	    /// libNVVM failed (load, symbol resolution, or compile call). Forwards
156	    /// the underlying [`NvvmError`].
157	    #[error("libnvvm: {0}")]
158	    Nvvm(#[from] NvvmError),
159	
160	    /// nvJitLink failed (load, symbol resolution, or link call). Forwards
161	    /// the underlying [`NvJitLinkError`].
162	    #[error("nvJitLink: {0}")]
163	    NvJitLink(#[from] NvJitLinkError),
164	
165	    /// `libdevice.10.bc` could not be located. `tried` lists every path
166	    /// that was probed, in order, joined by newlines.
167	    #[error(
168	        "Could not locate libdevice.10.bc. Set CUDA_OXIDE_LIBDEVICE, CUDA_TOOLKIT_PATH, or CUDA_HOME, or install the CUDA Toolkit. Tried:\n  {tried}"
169	    )]
170	    LibdeviceNotFound {
171	        /// Newline-joined list of paths that were probed.
172	        tried: String,
173	    },
174	
175	    /// Reading or writing one of the pipeline artifacts (`.ll`,
176	    /// `libdevice.10.bc`, `.ltoir`, `.cubin`) failed.
177	    #[error("Failed reading {path}: {source}")]
178	    Io {
179	        /// Path of the file that could not be read or written.
180	        path: PathBuf,
181	        /// Underlying I/O error.
182	        #[source]
183	        source: std::io::Error,
184	    },
185	
186	    /// [`load_kernel_module`] could not find a supported artifact in the
187	    /// binary's manifest directory.
188	    #[error(
189	        "Could not find any kernel artifact for {name} in {dir}. \
190	         Looked for {name}.cubin, {name}.ptx, {name}.ll, {name}.ltoir. \
191	         Did `cargo oxide run` complete successfully?"
192	    )]
193	    NoArtifact {
194	        /// Kernel artifact stem that was looked up.
195	        name: String,
196	        /// Directory that was searched.
197	        dir: PathBuf,
198	    },
199	
200	    /// `cuModuleLoad` (or another driver call) returned an error after the
201	    /// pipeline produced a cubin.
202	    #[error("CUDA driver: {0}")]
203	    Driver(#[from] DriverError),
204	}

207	// Build (NVVM IR + libdevice -> LTOIR -> cubin)
208	// ============================================================================
209	
210	/// Compile NVVM IR at `ll_path` to a cubin and return its path.
211	///
212	/// Steps:
213	/// 1. Read `ll_path` (NVVM IR text) and the libdevice bitcode (located via
214	///    [`find_libdevice`]).
215	/// 2. Reuse a verified content-addressed entry when every semantic input and
216	///    the exact loaded CUDA tool binaries match. Otherwise compile via
217	///    libNVVM and link via nvJitLink.
218	/// 3. Write compatibility copies next to `ll_path` as `<stem>.ltoir` and
219	///    `<stem>.cubin`, and return the sibling cubin path.
220	///
221	/// `arch` must be a concrete `sm_XX` or `compute_XX` CUDA target. It is
222	/// normalized to `compute_XX` for libNVVM and `sm_XX` for nvJitLink.
223	///
224	/// The cache lives below `.oxide-artifacts/ltoir-cubin-cache/v1` and is skipped
225	/// when the exact loaded `libnvvm.so` or `libnvJitLink.so` cannot be identified.
226	/// Cache failures never prevent a fresh build.
227	pub fn build_cubin_from_ll(ll_path: &Path, arch: &str) -> Result<PathBuf, LtoirError> {
228	    let arch: CudaArch = arch.parse()?;
229	    Ok(build_cubin_from_ll_file(ll_path, &arch)?.path)
230	}
231	
232	struct FileCubin {
233	    path: PathBuf,
234	    image: Vec<u8>,
235	}
236	
237	fn build_cubin_from_ll_file(ll_path: &Path, arch: &CudaArch) -> Result<FileCubin, LtoirError> {
238	    // Read the source before writing target metadata or derived artifacts.
239	    let ll_bytes = std::fs::read(ll_path).map_err(|source| LtoirError::Io {
240	        path: ll_path.to_path_buf(),
241	        source,
242	    })?;
243	    validate_ir_target_sidecar(ll_path, arch)?;
244	    // Record the supplied target for older or manually created `.ll` files.
245	    // The sibling `.ltoir` can then be loaded after the `.ll` is removed.
246	    write_artifact_target_sidecar(ll_path, arch)?;
247	
248	    let stem = ll_path
249	        .file_stem()
250	        .and_then(|s| s.to_str())
251	        .unwrap_or("kernel");
252	    let dir = ll_path.parent().unwrap_or_else(|| Path::new("."));
253	    let ltoir_path = dir.join(format!("{stem}.ltoir"));
254	    let cubin_path = dir.join(format!("{stem}.cubin"));
255	
256	    let cached = cached_nvvm_ir_to_cubin(
257	        dir,
258	        &ll_bytes,
259	        &ll_path.display().to_string(),
260	        &ltoir_path.display().to_string(),
261	        arch,
262	    )?;
263	    let ltoir = cached
264	        .ltoir
265	        .as_deref()
266	        .expect("NVVM IR cache results always include the generated LTOIR");
267	
268	    std::fs::write(&ltoir_path, ltoir).map_err(|source| LtoirError::Io {
269	        path: ltoir_path.clone(),
270	        source,
271	    })?;
272	
273	    // Keep the historical sibling output for tools and callers that inspect
274	    // it, but never load it after checking the cache: another process may
275	    // replace a mutable sibling between those two operations.
276	    std::fs::write(&cubin_path, &cached.cubin).map_err(|source| LtoirError::Io {
277	        path: cubin_path.clone(),
278	        source,
279	    })?;
280	
281	    Ok(FileCubin {
282	        path: cubin_path,
283	        image: cached.cubin,
284	    })
285	}

288	///
289	/// This is the embedded-artifact counterpart of [`build_cubin_from_ll`]. It
290	/// adds `libdevice.10.bc`, asks libNVVM for LTOIR, links that LTOIR with
291	/// nvJitLink, and returns the final cubin bytes without creating sidecar files.
292	pub fn build_cubin_from_nvvm_ir(
293	    nvvm_ir: &[u8],
294	    module_name: &str,
295	    arch: &str,
296	) -> Result<Vec<u8>, LtoirError> {

299	    let ltoir_name = format!("{module_name}.ltoir");
300	    link_ltoir_to_cubin_parsed(&ltoir, &ltoir_name, &arch)
301	}

352	    nvj: &LibNvJitLink,
353	    ltoir: &[u8],
354	    module_name: &str,
355	    arch: &CudaArch,
356	) -> Result<Vec<u8>, LtoirError> {

384	fn compile_nvvm_ir_to_ltoir_parsed(
385	    nvvm_ir: &[u8],
386	    module_name: &str,
387	    arch: &CudaArch,
388	) -> Result<Vec<u8>, LtoirError> {
389	    let libdevice_bytes = read_libdevice_bytes()?;
390	    let nvvm = LibNvvm::load()?;
391	    validate_nvvm_frontend(&nvvm, arch)?;
392	    compile_nvvm_ir_to_ltoir_with(&nvvm, nvvm_ir, module_name, arch, &libdevice_bytes)
393	}
394	
395	fn read_libdevice_bytes() -> Result<Vec<u8>, LtoirError> {
396	    let path = find_libdevice()?;
397	    std::fs::read(&path).map_err(|source| LtoirError::Io { path, source })
398	}
399	
400	fn validate_nvvm_frontend(nvvm: &LibNvvm, arch: &CudaArch) -> Result<(), LtoirError> {
401	    let ir_version = nvvm.ir_version()?;
402	    if (ir_version.ir_major, ir_version.ir_minor) != (2, 0) {
403	        return Err(LtoirError::UnsupportedNvvmIrVersion {
404	            major: ir_version.ir_major,
405	            minor: ir_version.ir_minor,
406	        });
407	    }
408	    if let Some(llvm_major) = nvvm.llvm_version(arch)? {
409	        let mismatch = if arch.uses_legacy_llvm() {
410	            llvm_major != 7
411	        } else {
412	            llvm_major == 7
413	        };
414	        if mismatch {
415	            return Err(LtoirError::DialectMismatch {
416	                target: arch.compute(),
417	                llvm_major,
418	                expected: if arch.uses_legacy_llvm() {
419	                    "legacy LLVM 7"
420	                } else {
421	                    "modern opaque-pointer"
422	                },
423	            });
424	        }
425	    }
426	    Ok(())
427	}
428	
429	fn compile_nvvm_ir_to_ltoir_with(
430	    nvvm: &LibNvvm,
431	    nvvm_ir: &[u8],
432	    module_name: &str,
433	    arch: &CudaArch,
434	    libdevice_bytes: &[u8],
435	) -> Result<Vec<u8>, LtoirError> {
436	    let mut prog = Program::new(nvvm)?;
437	    // Add libdevice first so the kernel module's __nv_* references are
438	    // resolved at compile time. Order doesn't strictly matter -- libNVVM
439	    // does its own symbol resolution -- but this matches the pattern used
440	    // by NVCC and the device_ffi_test C tools.
441	    prog.add_module(libdevice_bytes, "libdevice.10.bc")?;
442	    prog.add_module(nvvm_ir, module_name)?;
443	
444	    let arch_opt = format!("-arch={}", arch.compute());
445	    prog.verify(&[&arch_opt])?;
446	    Ok(prog.compile(&[&arch_opt, "-gen-lto"])?)
447	}
448	
449	fn cached_nvvm_ir_to_cubin(
450	    source_dir: &Path,
451	    nvvm_ir: &[u8],
452	    nvvm_module_name: &str,
453	    ltoir_module_name: &str,
454	    arch: &CudaArch,
455	) -> Result<CacheResult, LtoirError> {
456	    let libdevice = read_libdevice_bytes()?;
457	    let nvvm = load_nvvm_tool()?;
458	    validate_nvvm_frontend(&nvvm.library, arch)?;
459	    let nvj = load_nvjitlink_tool()?;
460	
461	    let key = nvvm.digest.and_then(|nvvm_digest| {
462	        nvj.digest.map(|nvjitlink_digest| {
463	            nvvm_ir_cubin_cache_key(
464	                nvvm_ir,
465	                nvvm_module_name,
466	                ltoir_module_name,
467	                arch,
468	                &libdevice,
469	                &nvvm_digest,
470	                &nvjitlink_digest,
471	            )
472	        })
473	    });
474	
475	    let build = || -> Result<BuiltArtifacts, LtoirError> {
476	        let ltoir = compile_nvvm_ir_to_ltoir_with(
477	            &nvvm.library,
478	            nvvm_ir,
479	            nvvm_module_name,
480	            arch,
481	            &libdevice,
482	        )?;
483	        let cubin = link_ltoir_to_cubin_with(&nvj.library, &ltoir, ltoir_module_name, arch)?;
484	        Ok(BuiltArtifacts::new(cubin, Some(ltoir)))
485	    };
486	
487	    let result = match key {
488	        Some(key) => cache_or_build(source_dir, &key, build)?,
489	        None => uncached_result(build()?),
490	    };
491	    report_cache_result(&result);
492	    Ok(result)
493	}
494	
495	fn cached_ltoir_to_cubin(
496	    source_dir: &Path,
497	    ltoir: &[u8],
498	    module_name: &str,
499	    arch: &CudaArch,
500	) -> Result<CacheResult, LtoirError> {
501	    let nvj = load_nvjitlink_tool()?;
502	    let key = nvj
503	        .digest
504	        .map(|digest| ltoir_cubin_cache_key(ltoir, module_name, arch, &digest));
505	    let build = || -> Result<BuiltArtifacts, LtoirError> {
506	        link_ltoir_to_cubin_with(&nvj.library, ltoir, module_name, arch)
507	            .map(|cubin| BuiltArtifacts::new(cubin, None))
508	    };
509	
510	    let result = match key {
511	        Some(key) => cache_or_build(source_dir, &key, build)?,
512	        None => uncached_result(build()?),
513	    };
514	    report_cache_result(&result);
515	    Ok(result)
516	}
517	
518	fn load_nvvm_tool() -> Result<Arc<LoadedNvvmTool>, LtoirError> {
519	    if let Some(loaded) = NVVM_TOOL.get() {
520	        return Ok(Arc::clone(loaded));
521	    }
522	    let _load_guard = NVVM_TOOL_LOAD
523	        .get_or_init(|| Mutex::new(()))
524	        .lock()
525	        .unwrap_or_else(|poisoned| poisoned.into_inner());
526	    if let Some(loaded) = NVVM_TOOL.get() {
527	        return Ok(Arc::clone(loaded));
528	    }
529	
530	    let library = LibNvvm::load_for_cache()?;
531	    let digest = loaded_nvvm_digest(&library);
532	    let loaded = Arc::new(LoadedNvvmTool {
533	        library: Arc::new(library),
534	        digest,
535	    });
536	    let _ = NVVM_TOOL.set(Arc::clone(&loaded));
537	    Ok(loaded)
538	}

541	    if let Some(loaded) = NVJITLINK_TOOL.get() {

543	    }
544	    let _load_guard = NVJITLINK_TOOL_LOAD
545	        .get_or_init(|| Mutex::new(()))
546	        .lock()
547	        .unwrap_or_else(|poisoned| poisoned.into_inner());
548	    if let Some(loaded) = NVJITLINK_TOOL.get() {
549	        return Ok(Arc::clone(loaded));
550	    }
551	
552	    let library = LibNvJitLink::load_for_cache()?;
553	    let digest = loaded_nvjitlink_digest(&library);
554	    let loaded = Arc::new(LoadedNvJitLinkTool {
555	        library: Arc::new(library),
556	        digest,
557	    });
558	    let _ = NVJITLINK_TOOL.set(Arc::clone(&loaded));
559	    Ok(loaded)
560	}

562	fn loaded_nvvm_digest(nvvm: &LibNvvm) -> Option<[u8; 32]> {
563	    let file = nvvm.loaded_file_if_unchanged();
564	    let digest = loaded_tool_digest("libNVVM", file)?;
565	    if nvvm.loaded_file_if_unchanged().is_some() {
566	        Some(digest)
567	    } else {
568	        report_changed_tool("libNVVM");
569	        None
570	    }
571	}

573	fn loaded_nvjitlink_digest(nvj: &LibNvJitLink) -> Option<[u8; 32]> {
574	    let file = nvj.loaded_file_if_unchanged();
575	    let digest = loaded_tool_digest("nvJitLink", file)?;
576	    if nvj.loaded_file_if_unchanged().is_some() {
577	        Some(digest)
578	    } else {
579	        report_changed_tool("nvJitLink");
580	        None
581	    }
582	}
583	
584	fn loaded_tool_digest(label: &str, file: Option<&std::fs::File>) -> Option<[u8; 32]> {
585	    let Some(file) = file else {
586	        if std::env::var_os("CUDA_OXIDE_VERBOSE").is_some() {
587	            eprintln!(
588	                "cuda-oxide: {label} has no exact loaded-file identity; bypassing the cubin cache"
589	            );
590	        }
591	        return None;
592	    };
593	
594	    match digest_file_handle(file) {
595	        Ok(digest) => Some(digest),
596	        Err(error) => {
597	            if std::env::var_os("CUDA_OXIDE_VERBOSE").is_some() {
598	                eprintln!(
599	                    "cuda-oxide: could not fingerprint the loaded {label} file ({error}); bypassing the cubin cache"
600	                );
601	            }
602	            None
603	        }
604	    }
605	}
606	
607	fn report_changed_tool(label: &str) {
608	    if std::env::var_os("CUDA_OXIDE_VERBOSE").is_some() {
609	        eprintln!(
610	            "cuda-oxide: {label} changed while it was fingerprinted; bypassing the cubin cache"
611	        );
612	    }
613	}
614	
615	fn nvvm_ir_cubin_cache_key(
616	    nvvm_ir: &[u8],
617	    nvvm_module_name: &str,
618	    ltoir_module_name: &str,
619	    arch: &CudaArch,
620	    libdevice: &[u8],
621	    libnvvm_digest: &[u8; 32],
622	    nvjitlink_digest: &[u8; 32],
623	) -> [u8; 32] {
624	    let nvvm_arch = format!("-arch={}", arch.compute());
625	    let linker_arch = format!("-arch={}", arch.sm());
626	    CacheKeyBuilder::new()
627	        .field("recipe", CACHE_RECIPE)
628	        .field("route", b"nvvm-ir-to-native-cubin")
629	        .field("output", b"elf-cubin")
630	        .field("nvvm-ir", nvvm_ir)
631	        .field("nvvm-module-name", nvvm_module_name.as_bytes())
632	        .field("libdevice", libdevice)
633	        .field("libdevice-module-name", b"libdevice.10.bc")
634	        .field("module-order", b"libdevice,nvvm-ir")
635	        .field("nvvm-verify-option", nvvm_arch.as_bytes())
636	        .field("nvvm-compile-option", nvvm_arch.as_bytes())
637	        .field("nvvm-compile-option", b"-gen-lto")
638	        .field("nvjitlink-input-kind", b"ltoir")
639	        .field("nvjitlink-module-name", ltoir_module_name.as_bytes())
640	        .field("nvjitlink-option", linker_arch.as_bytes())
641	        .field("nvjitlink-option", b"-lto")
642	        .field("libnvvm-sha256", libnvvm_digest)
643	        .field("libnvjitlink-sha256", nvjitlink_digest)
644	        .finish()
645	}

689	// ============================================================================
690	// Convenience: pick the right artifact and load it
691	// ============================================================================
692	
693	/// Convenience wrapper: load a kernel module by `name` from the binary's
694	/// own directory, building the cubin on demand if cuda-oxide emitted NVVM IR.
695	///
696	/// Lookup order, inside `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR` (the directory containing the
697	/// executable's `Cargo.toml`, where cuda-oxide writes its build artifacts):
698	///
699	/// 1. A target-recorded `<name>.ll` or `<name>.ltoir` -- `<name>.target`
700	///    identifies NVVM output and gives it precedence over older PTX.
701	/// 2. `<name>.ptx` -- the PTX path when no NVVM target file exists.
702	/// 3. An unrecorded `<name>.ll` / `<name>.ltoir` -- accepted only when
703	///    `CUDA_OXIDE_TARGET` explicitly supplies its original source target.
704	/// 4. `<name>.cubin` -- a standalone cubin when no source artifact exists.
705	///
706	/// If none of the four exist, returns [`LtoirError::NoArtifact`].
707	///
708	/// Use [`build_cubin_from_ll`] directly if you need explicit control over
709	/// the path or arch.
710	pub fn load_kernel_module(
711	    ctx: &Arc<CudaContext>,
712	    name: &str,
713	) -> Result<Arc<CudaModule>, LtoirError> {
714	    let dir = manifest_dir();
715	    let cubin = dir.join(format!("{name}.cubin"));
716	    let ptx = dir.join(format!("{name}.ptx"));
717	    let ll = dir.join(format!("{name}.ll"));
718	    let ltoir = dir.join(format!("{name}.ltoir"));
719	
720	    let has_recorded_nvvm_target =
721	        emitted_target_path(&ll)
722	            .try_exists()
723	            .map_err(|source| LtoirError::Io {
724	                path: emitted_target_path(&ll),
725	                source,
726	            })?;
727	    let selected = select_file_artifact(
728	        ptx.exists(),
729	        ll.exists(),
730	        ltoir.exists(),
731	        cubin.exists(),
732	        has_recorded_nvvm_target,
733	    )
734	    .ok_or_else(|| LtoirError::NoArtifact {
735	        name: name.to_string(),
736	        dir,
737	    })?;
738	
739	    match selected {
740	        FileArtifact::Ptx => Ok(ctx.load_module_from_file(
741	            ptx.to_str()
742	                .expect("kernel artifact path is not valid UTF-8"),
743	        )?),
744	        FileArtifact::NvvmIr => {
745	            let emitted = target_arch_for_artifact(&ll)?;
746	            let execution = execution_arch_for_context(ctx)?;
747	            match execution_route(&emitted, &execution)? {
748	                ExecutionRoute::Cubin => {
749	                    let cubin = build_cubin_from_ll_file(&ll, &emitted)?;
750	                    Ok(ctx.load_module_from_image(&cubin.image)?)
751	                }
752	                ExecutionRoute::PtxBridge => {
753	                    let nvvm_ir = read_artifact(&ll)?;
754	                    let ptx =
755	                        build_ptx_from_nvvm_ir(&nvvm_ir, &ll.display().to_string(), &emitted.sm())?;
756	                    Ok(ctx.load_module_from_image(&ptx)?)
757	                }
758	            }
759	        }
760	        FileArtifact::Ltoir => {
761	            let emitted = target_arch_for_artifact(&ltoir)?;
762	            let execution = execution_arch_for_context(ctx)?;
763	            let bytes = read_artifact(&ltoir)?;
764	            let image = match execution_route(&emitted, &execution)? {
765	                ExecutionRoute::Cubin => {
766	                    cached_ltoir_to_cubin(
767	                        ltoir.parent().unwrap_or_else(|| Path::new(".")),
768	                        &bytes,
769	                        &ltoir.display().to_string(),
770	                        &emitted,
771	                    )?
772	                    .cubin
773	                }
774	                ExecutionRoute::PtxBridge => {
775	                    link_ltoir_to_ptx_parsed(&bytes, &ltoir.display().to_string(), &emitted)?
776	                }
777	            };
778	            Ok(ctx.load_module_from_image(&image)?)
779	        }
780	        FileArtifact::Cubin => Ok(ctx.load_module_from_file(
781	            cubin
782	                .to_str()
783	                .expect("kernel artifact path is not valid UTF-8"),
784	        )?),
785	    }
786	}
787	
788	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
789	enum FileArtifact {
790	    Ptx,
791	    NvvmIr,
792	    Ltoir,
793	    Cubin,
794	}
795	
796	/// Select one file artifact. A `.target` file identifies NVVM IR/LTOIR as the
797	/// current output; without one, PTX takes precedence.
798	fn select_file_artifact(
799	    has_ptx: bool,
800	    has_nvvm_ir: bool,
801	    has_ltoir: bool,
802	    has_cubin: bool,
803	    has_recorded_nvvm_target: bool,
804	) -> Option<FileArtifact> {
805	    if has_recorded_nvvm_target {
806	        if has_nvvm_ir {
807	            return Some(FileArtifact::NvvmIr);
808	        }
809	        if has_ltoir {
810	            return Some(FileArtifact::Ltoir);
811	        }
812	        // The target file identifies NVVM output, but that output is missing.
813	        // Do not fall back to an older PTX or cubin.
814	        return None;
815	    }
816	
817	    if has_ptx {
818	        Some(FileArtifact::Ptx)
819	    } else if has_nvvm_ir {
820	        // Older or manual NVVM IR requires an explicit CUDA_OXIDE_TARGET.
821	        Some(FileArtifact::NvvmIr)
822	    } else if has_ltoir {
823	        Some(FileArtifact::Ltoir)
824	    } else if has_cubin {
825	        Some(FileArtifact::Cubin)
826	    } else {
827	        None
828	    }
829	}

835	/// Locate `libdevice.10.bc` from the CUDA Toolkit.
836	///
837	/// Search order:
838	/// 1. `CUDA_OXIDE_LIBDEVICE` env var (used as-is if it points to an
839	///    existing file).
840	/// 2. `<root>/nvvm/libdevice/libdevice.10.bc` for `<root>` in
841	///    `CUDA_TOOLKIT_PATH`, `CUDA_HOME`, `CUDA_PATH`, `/usr/local/cuda`,
842	///    `/opt/cuda`.
843	///
844	/// Returns [`LtoirError::LibdeviceNotFound`] with the full list of probed
845	/// paths if nothing matches.
846	///
847	/// Thin wrapper over [`libnvvm_sys::find_libdevice`], which owns the probe
848	/// (libdevice ships in the toolkit's `nvvm/` component next to `libnvvm.so`).
849	pub fn find_libdevice() -> Result<PathBuf, LtoirError> {
850	    libnvvm_sys::find_libdevice()
851	        .map_err(|libnvvm_sys::LibdeviceNotFound { tried }| LtoirError::LibdeviceNotFound { tried })
852	}

854	/// Read and validate an explicit source target from `CUDA_OXIDE_TARGET`.
855	///
856	/// Artifact loaders prefer the target recorded with the artifact. Older
857	/// artifacts without a `.target` file require this explicit value because the
858	/// current GPU does not reveal which target was used to produce existing IR.
859	pub fn target_arch() -> Result<String, LtoirError> {
860	    let explicit = std::env::var("CUDA_OXIDE_TARGET").ok();
861	    resolve_source_target(None, explicit.as_deref()).map(|target| target.sm())
862	}

864	/// Query the physical execution GPU. Target-related environment variables
865	/// describe the input artifact, not the GPU that will execute it.
866	pub(crate) fn execution_arch_for_context(ctx: &CudaContext) -> Result<CudaArch, LtoirError> {
867	    let (major, minor) = ctx.compute_capability()?;
868	    format!("sm_{major}{minor}")
869	        .parse::<CudaArch>()
870	        .map_err(LtoirError::from)
871	}

873	/// Directory to search for kernel artifacts (`.cubin` / `.ptx` / `.ll` /
874	/// `.ltoir`).
875	///
876	/// Reads `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR`, which `cargo run` sets to the directory of
877	/// the executable's `Cargo.toml` -- the same directory cuda-oxide writes
878	/// its build artifacts to. Falls back to the current working directory if
879	/// the env var is unset (e.g. when the binary is launched outside cargo).
880	///
881	/// Note: `env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")` cannot be used here because it
882	/// resolves to *this* crate's manifest dir at compile time, not the
883	/// downstream binary's.
884	fn manifest_dir() -> PathBuf {
885	    if let Ok(d) = std::env::var("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR") {
886	        return PathBuf::from(d);
887	    }
888	    std::env::current_dir().unwrap_or_else(|_| PathBuf::from("."))
889	}

895	fn target_arch_for_artifact(artifact_path: &Path) -> Result<CudaArch, LtoirError> {
896	    let explicit = std::env::var("CUDA_OXIDE_TARGET").ok();
897	    target_arch_for_artifact_with_explicit(artifact_path, explicit.as_deref())

899	
900	fn target_arch_for_artifact_with_explicit(
901	    artifact_path: &Path,
902	    explicit_target: Option<&str>,
903	) -> Result<CudaArch, LtoirError> {
904	    resolve_source_target(read_emitted_target(artifact_path)?, explicit_target)
905	}

907	/// Resolve the original target from artifact metadata or an explicit value.
908	/// The execution GPU is not used to infer the artifact's target.
909	pub(crate) fn resolve_source_target(
910	    recorded_target: Option<CudaArch>,
911	    explicit_target: Option<&str>,
912	) -> Result<CudaArch, LtoirError> {
913	    if let Some(target) = recorded_target {
914	        return Ok(target);
915	    }
916	    explicit_target
917	        .ok_or(LtoirError::TargetNotFound)?
918	        .parse()
919	        .map_err(LtoirError::from)
920	}
921	
922	fn emitted_target_path(ll_path: &Path) -> PathBuf {
923	    ll_path.with_extension("target")
924	}
925	
926	fn read_emitted_target(ll_path: &Path) -> Result<Option<CudaArch>, LtoirError> {
927	    let path = emitted_target_path(ll_path);
928	    match std::fs::read_to_string(&path) {
929	        Ok(target) => Ok(Some(target.trim().parse()?)),
930	        Err(source) if source.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => Ok(None),
931	        Err(source) => Err(LtoirError::Io { path, source }),
932	    }
933	}
934	
935	fn validate_ir_target_sidecar(ll_path: &Path, requested: &CudaArch) -> Result<(), LtoirError> {
936	    let Some(emitted) = read_emitted_target(ll_path)? else {
937	        return Ok(());
938	    };
939	    if emitted != *requested {
940	        return Err(LtoirError::TargetMismatch {
941	            ir_path: ll_path.to_path_buf(),
942	            emitted: emitted.sm(),
943	            requested: requested.sm(),
944	        });
945	    }
946	    Ok(())
947	}
948	
949	fn write_artifact_target_sidecar(
950	    artifact_path: &Path,
951	    target: &CudaArch,
952	) -> Result<(), LtoirError> {
953	    let path = emitted_target_path(artifact_path);
954	    std::fs::write(&path, format!("{}\n", target.sm()))
955	        .map_err(|source| LtoirError::Io { path, source })
956	}
957	
958	fn read_artifact(path: &Path) -> Result<Vec<u8>, LtoirError> {
959	    std::fs::read(path).map_err(|source| LtoirError::Io {
960	        path: path.to_path_buf(),
961	        source,
962	    })
963	}

965	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
966	pub(crate) enum ExecutionRoute {
967	    /// Produce native code for the architecture that emitted the IR.
968	    Cubin,
969	    /// Keep the original virtual architecture in PTX and let the driver JIT it
970	    /// for the newer GPU.
971	    PtxBridge,
972	}
973	
974	/// Select an execution route without changing either architecture.
975	pub(crate) fn execution_route(
976	    emitted: &CudaArch,
977	    execution: &CudaArch,
978	) -> Result<ExecutionRoute, LtoirError> {
979	    if emitted.capability() == execution.capability() {
980	        return Ok(ExecutionRoute::Cubin);
981	    }
982	
983	    let incompatible = |reason| LtoirError::IncompatibleExecutionTarget {
984	        emitted: emitted.sm(),
985	        execution: execution.sm(),
986	        reason,
987	    };
988	
989	    if emitted.suffix().is_some() {
990	        return Err(incompatible(
991	            "targets with an architecture suffix, such as sm_90a, cannot be forwarded to a different GPU",
992	        ));
993	    }
994	    if emitted.capability() > execution.capability() {
995	        return Err(incompatible(
996	            "an artifact built for a newer GPU cannot run on an older GPU",
997	        ));
998	    }
999	    if emitted.uses_legacy_llvm() && execution.capability() >= 100 {
1000	        return Ok(ExecutionRoute::PtxBridge);
1001	    }
1002	
1003	    Err(incompatible(
1004	        "only standard pre-Blackwell targets, such as sm_86, can be converted to PTX for Blackwell",
1005	    ))
1006	}

14	/// GPU printf macro for formatted output from GPU kernels.
15	///
16	/// This macro translates Rust-style format strings to C-style and calls
17	/// CUDA's `vprintf` function.
18	///
19	/// # Usage
20	///
21	/// ```ignore
22	/// use cuda_device::gpu_printf;
23	///
24	/// #[kernel]
25	/// fn my_kernel() {
26	///     let tid = thread::index_1d().get();
27	///     gpu_printf!("Thread {}: Hello from GPU!\n", tid);
28	/// }
29	/// ```
30	///
31	/// # Format Specifiers
32	///
33	/// | Specifier | Description     | Example                                        |
34	/// |-----------|-----------------|------------------------------------------------|
35	/// | `{}`      | Default format  | `gpu_printf!("{}", 42)`                        |
36	/// | `{:x}`    | Hex (lower)     | `gpu_printf!("{:x}", 255)` → "ff"              |
37	/// | `{:X}`    | Hex (upper)     | `gpu_printf!("{:X}", 255)` → "FF"              |
38	/// | `{:#x}`   | Hex with prefix | `gpu_printf!("{:#x}", 255)` → "0xff"           |
39	/// | `{:o}`    | Octal           | `gpu_printf!("{:o}", 8)` → "10"                |
40	/// | `{:e}`    | Scientific      | `gpu_printf!("{:e}", 1000.0)` → "1.000000e+03" |
41	/// | `{:.N}`   | Precision       | `gpu_printf!("{:.2}", 3.14159)` → "3.14"       |
42	/// | `{:N}`    | Width           | `gpu_printf!("{:8}", 42)` → "      42"         |
43	/// | `{:0N}`   | Zero-pad        | `gpu_printf!("{:08}", 42)` → "00000042"        |
44	///
45	/// # Returns
46	///
47	/// The number of arguments (i32), or negative on error.
48	/// Note: CUDA vprintf returns arg count, not character count.
49	#[proc_macro]
50	pub fn gpu_printf(input: TokenStream) -> TokenStream {
51	    let input = syn::parse_macro_input!(input as printf::GpuPrintfInput);
52	    printf::gpu_printf_impl(input).into()
53	}

55	/// Inline PTX assembly for CUDA device code.
56	///
57	/// The macro accepts the `%0` operand placeholders used by CUDA inline PTX, plus
58	/// `in` / `out` operands with PTX constraint strings. Supported constraints are
59	/// CUDA register constraints `"h"`, `"r"`, `"l"`, `"q"`, `"f"`, and `"d"`, plus
60	/// `"n"` for immediate integer constants:
61	///
62	/// ```ignore
63	/// let y: u32;
64	/// unsafe {
65	///     ptx_asm!(
66	///         "add.u32 %0, %1, %2;",
67	///         out("=r") y,
68	///         in("r") x,
69	///         in("r") z,
70	///     );
71	/// }
72	/// ```
73	///
74	/// Literal PTX registers that begin with `%` must be escaped as `%%`, matching
75	/// CUDA C++ inline PTX. Literal `$` labels can be written normally.
76	///
77	/// The initial surface supports zero or one `out`, up to 16 `in` operands,
78	/// `clobber("memory")`, `options(register_only)`, and the explicit
79	/// `options(register_only, may_diverge)` opt-in.
80	///
81	/// By default, snippets are treated as side-effecting and stay inside their
82	/// current control flow. Use `options(register_only)` only for snippets that
83	/// read explicit operands and write the explicit output. **Never** use
84	/// `may_diverge` for `.sync` instructions, collectives, or any snippet whose
85	/// participating lanes matter.
86	#[proc_macro]
87	pub fn ptx_asm(input: TokenStream) -> TokenStream {
88	    let input = syn::parse_macro_input!(input as ptx_asm::PtxAsmInput);
89	    ptx_asm::ptx_asm_impl(input).into()
90	}

167	/// Reject function names that start with the reserved cuda-oxide prefix
168	/// (`cuda_oxide_`).
169	///
170	/// User code must not define functions in the cuda-oxide internal naming
171	/// namespace. Two failure modes this guards against:
172	///
173	/// 1. **Cosmetic.** `#[kernel] fn cuda_oxide_kernel_foo()` would expand to
174	///    a doubly-nested name like
175	///    `fn cuda_oxide_kernel_<hash>_cuda_oxide_kernel_foo()`, producing
176	///    confusing symbol names in MIR dumps and stack traces.
177	/// 2. **Forward-compatibility.** Future refactors may extend the namespace;
178	///    rejecting it at the source level keeps the contract clean.
179	///
180	/// Returns `Some(compile_error)` to be returned from the macro entry point,
181	/// or `None` if the name is safe.
182	fn reject_reserved_name(name: &Ident) -> Option<TokenStream> {
183	    let name_str = name.to_string();
184	    if name_str.starts_with(RESERVED_ROOT) {
185	        let msg = format!(
186	            "function name `{name_str}` starts with the reserved cuda-oxide \
187	             prefix `{RESERVED_ROOT}`; rename your function — this namespace \
188	             is reserved for cuda-oxide internal symbol mangling \
189	             (see crates/reserved-oxide-symbols)"
190	        );
191	        Some(syn::Error::new(name.span(), msg).to_compile_error().into())
192	    } else {
193	        None
194	    }
195	}

197	/// Reject argument-position `impl Trait`, which rustc represents as a hidden
198	/// type parameter that procedural macros cannot name at launch sites.
199	///
200	/// Without this check the host emits a non-generic lookup name while the
201	/// backend correctly emits a `_TID_...` specialization, causing a runtime
202	/// function-not-found error. A named generic preserves the same source-level
203	/// intent and gives both sides an explicit specialization identity.
204	fn impl_trait_parameter_error(input: &ItemFn, item_kind: &str) -> Option<syn::Error> {
205	    #[derive(Default)]
206	    struct Finder {
207	        first: Option<syn::TypeImplTrait>,
208	    }
209	
210	    impl<'ast> syn::visit::Visit<'ast> for Finder {
211	        fn visit_type_impl_trait(&mut self, node: &'ast syn::TypeImplTrait) {
212	            if self.first.is_none() {
213	                self.first = Some(node.clone());
214	            }
215	        }
216	    }
217	
218	    let mut finder = Finder::default();
219	    for arg in &input.sig.inputs {
220	        if let FnArg::Typed(pat_type) = arg {
221	            syn::visit::Visit::visit_type(&mut finder, &pat_type.ty);
222	        }
223	    }
224	
225	    finder.first.map(|impl_trait| {
226	        syn::Error::new_spanned(
227	            impl_trait,
228	            format!(
229	                "{item_kind} parameters cannot use `impl Trait`; name the type parameter explicitly (for example, `fn named<T: Trait>(value: T)`) so host and device specialization identities agree"
230	            ),
231	        )
232	    })
233	}

263	/// async methods. Kernel parameter types are mapped to host-side launch types:
264	///
265	/// - `&[T]` -> `&cuda_core::DeviceBuffer<T>`
266	/// - `&mut [T]` -> `&mut cuda_core::DeviceBuffer<T>`
267	/// - `DisjointSlice<T>` -> `&mut cuda_core::DeviceBuffer<T>`
268	/// - `Copy` scalar/struct/closure/raw-pointer arguments keep their original
269	///   type and pass through `cuda_host::KernelScalar`
270	///
271	/// # Example
272	///
273	/// ```ignore
274	/// #[cuda_module]
275	/// mod kernels {
276	///     use super::*;
277	///
278	///     #[kernel]
279	///     pub fn vecadd(a: &[f32], b: &[f32], mut c: DisjointSlice<f32>) {
280	///         // ...
281	///     }
282	/// }
283	///
284	/// let module = kernels::load(&ctx)?;
285	/// module.vecadd(&stream, LaunchConfig::for_num_elems(n), &a, &b, &mut c)?;
286	///
287	/// let module = kernels::load_async(0)?;
288	/// module.vecadd_async(LaunchConfig::for_num_elems(n), &a, &b, &mut c)?.sync()?;
289	///
290	/// let (a, b, c) = module
291	///     .vecadd_async_owned(LaunchConfig::for_num_elems(n), a, b, c)?
292	///     .await?;
293	/// ```
294	#[proc_macro_attribute]
295	pub fn cuda_module(attr: TokenStream, item: TokenStream) -> TokenStream {
296	    if !attr.is_empty() {
297	        return syn::Error::new(
298	            proc_macro2::Span::call_site(),
299	            "cuda_module does not take arguments yet",
300	        )
301	        .to_compile_error()
302	        .into();
303	    }
304	
305	    let input = parse_macro_input!(item as ItemMod);
306	    match expand_cuda_module(input) {
307	        Ok(tokens) => tokens.into(),
308	        Err(error) => error.to_compile_error().into(),
309	    }
310	}

338	fn expand_cuda_module(module: ItemMod) -> syn::Result<TokenStream2> {
339	    let module_attrs = &module.attrs;
340	    let vis = &module.vis;
341	    let ident = &module.ident;
342	    let Some((_brace, items)) = &module.content else {
343	        return Err(syn::Error::new_spanned(
344	            &module.ident,
345	            "cuda_module requires an inline module so kernel signatures are visible",
346	        ));
347	    };
348	
349	    let kernels = collect_cuda_module_kernels(items)?;
350	    if kernels.is_empty() {
351	        return Err(syn::Error::new_spanned(
352	            &module.ident,
353	            "cuda_module found no #[kernel] functions in this module",
354	        ));
355	    }
356	    let constants = collect_cuda_module_constants(items, ident)?;
357	    let module_items = cuda_module_items_with_constant_symbols(items, &constants);
358	
359	    let non_generic_kernels = kernels.iter().filter(|kernel| !kernel.is_generic);
360	    let function_fields = non_generic_kernels.clone().map(|kernel| {
361	        let cfg_attrs = &kernel.cfg_attrs;
362	        let field = cuda_module_function_field(&kernel.fn_name);
363	        quote! {
364	            #(#cfg_attrs)*
365	            #field: ::cuda_core::CudaFunction,
366	        }
367	    });
368	
369	    let function_initializers = non_generic_kernels.map(|kernel| {
370	        let cfg_attrs = &kernel.cfg_attrs;
371	        let field = cuda_module_function_field(&kernel.fn_name);
372	        let marker = cuda_kernel_marker_name(&kernel.fn_name);
373	        quote! {
374	            #(#cfg_attrs)*
375	            #field: module.load_function(<#marker as ::cuda_host::CudaKernel>::PTX_NAME)?,
376	        }
377	    });
378	
379	    let artifact_anchor_statements = cuda_module_artifact_anchor_statements(&kernels)?;
380	    let has_generic = kernels.iter().any(|k| k.is_generic);
381	    let module_loader = if has_generic {
382	        // At least one kernel is generic: its PTX is emitted into the
383	        // consuming binary's bundle, not this crate's bundle. Merge all
384	        // PTX bundles from the executable so generic monomorphizations are
385	        // visible regardless of which crate compiled them.
386	        quote! {
387	            let _ = name; // merged load ignores the crate-name hint
388	            let module = ::cuda_host::load_all_ptx_bundles_merged(ctx)?;
389	        }
390	    } else {
391	        quote! {
392	            let module = ::cuda_host::load_embedded_module(ctx, name)?;
393	        }
394	    };
395	    let constant_fields = constants.iter().map(generate_cuda_module_constant_field);
396	    let constant_initializers = constants
397	        .iter()
398	        .map(generate_cuda_module_constant_initializer);
399	    let launch_methods = kernels.iter().map(generate_cuda_module_launch_method);
400	    let constant_resolver_methods = constants
401	        .iter()
402	        .map(generate_cuda_module_constant_resolver_method);
403	    let set_constant_methods = constants
404	        .iter()
405	        .map(generate_cuda_module_set_constant_method);
406	    let async_module_items = if cfg!(feature = "async") {
407	        quote! {
408	            pub fn load_async(
409	                device_id: usize,
410	            ) -> ::core::result::Result<LoadedModule, ::cuda_host::cuda_async::error::DeviceError> {
411	                load_async_named(device_id, env!("CARGO_PKG_NAME"))
412	            }
413	
414	            pub fn load_async_named(
415	                device_id: usize,
416	                name: &str,
417	            ) -> ::core::result::Result<LoadedModule, ::cuda_host::cuda_async::error::DeviceError> {
418	                ::cuda_host::load_cuda_module_from_async_context(device_id, |ctx| load_named(ctx, name))
419	            }
420	        }
421	    } else {
422	        TokenStream2::new()
423	    };
424	    let async_launch_methods = if cfg!(feature = "async") {
425	        let async_launch_methods = kernels.iter().map(generate_cuda_module_async_launch_method);
426	        let owned_async_launch_methods = kernels
427	            .iter()
428	            .map(generate_cuda_module_owned_async_launch_method);
429	        quote! {
430	            #(#async_launch_methods)*
431	            #(#owned_async_launch_methods)*
432	        }
433	    } else {
434	        TokenStream2::new()
435	    };
436	
437	    Ok(quote! {
438	        #(#module_attrs)*
439	        #vis mod #ident {
440	            #(#module_items)*
441	
442	            #[derive(Clone, Debug)]
443	            #[allow(non_snake_case)]
444	            pub struct LoadedModule {
445	                __module: ::std::sync::Arc<::cuda_core::CudaModule>,
446	                __generic_functions: ::std::sync::Arc<
447	                    ::std::sync::Mutex<
448	                        ::std::collections::HashMap<&'static str, ::cuda_core::CudaFunction>
449	                    >
450	                >,
451	                #(#function_fields)*
452	                #(#constant_fields)*
453	            }
454	
455	            pub fn load(
456	                ctx: &::std::sync::Arc<::cuda_core::CudaContext>,
457	            ) -> ::core::result::Result<LoadedModule, ::cuda_host::EmbeddedModuleError> {
458	                load_named(ctx, env!("CARGO_PKG_NAME"))
459	            }
460	
461	            pub fn load_named(
462	                ctx: &::std::sync::Arc<::cuda_core::CudaContext>,
463	                name: &str,
464	            ) -> ::core::result::Result<LoadedModule, ::cuda_host::EmbeddedModuleError> {
465	                #artifact_anchor_statements
466	                #module_loader
467	                from_module(module).map_err(::cuda_host::EmbeddedModuleError::Driver)
468	            }
469	
470	            pub fn from_module(
471	                module: ::std::sync::Arc<::cuda_core::CudaModule>,
472	            ) -> ::core::result::Result<LoadedModule, ::cuda_core::DriverError> {
473	                Ok(LoadedModule {
474	                    __module: module.clone(),
475	                    __generic_functions: ::std::sync::Arc::new(
476	                        ::std::sync::Mutex::new(::std::collections::HashMap::new())
477	                    ),
478	                    #(#function_initializers)*
479	                    #(#constant_initializers)*
480	                })
481	            }
482	
483	            #async_module_items
484	
485	            impl LoadedModule {
486	                pub fn as_cuda_module(&self) -> &::std::sync::Arc<::cuda_core::CudaModule> {
487	                    &self.__module
488	                }
489	
490	                #(#launch_methods)*
491	                #(#constant_resolver_methods)*
492	                #(#set_constant_methods)*
493	                #async_launch_methods
494	            }
495	        }
496	    })
497	}

499	fn collect_cuda_module_kernels(items: &[Item]) -> syn::Result<Vec<CudaModuleKernel>> {
500	    let mut kernels = Vec::new();
501	    for item in items {
502	        let Item::Fn(item_fn) = item else {
503	            continue;
504	        };
505	        if !has_attr_named(&item_fn.attrs, "kernel") {
506	            continue;
507	        }
508	        if let Some(err) = impl_trait_parameter_error(item_fn, "kernel") {
509	            return Err(err);
510	        }
511	        let cluster_dim = cuda_module_cluster_dim(&item_fn.attrs)?;
512	        let cooperative = cuda_module_cooperative(&item_fn.attrs)?;
513	        let params = cuda_module_params(item_fn)?;
514	        let is_generic = has_codegen_generics(&item_fn.sig.generics);
515	        kernels.push(CudaModuleKernel {
516	            vis: item_fn.vis.clone(),
517	            cfg_attrs: cuda_module_cfg_attrs(&item_fn.attrs),
518	            method_attrs: cuda_module_method_attrs(&item_fn.attrs),
519	            unsafety: item_fn.sig.unsafety,
520	            fn_name: item_fn.sig.ident.clone(),
521	            generics: item_fn.sig.generics.clone(),
522	            params,
523	            cluster_dim,
524	            cooperative,
525	            is_generic,
526	        });
527	    }
528	    Ok(kernels)
529	}

531	/// Generate the statements that pin this crate's embedded device artifact
532	/// into the final binary.
533	///
534	/// The codegen backend stores each crate's compiled device code (PTX,
535	/// cubin, NVVM IR, or LTOIR) in a `.oxart` data section of a small extra
536	/// object file. When the crate that holds the `#[cuda_module]` is a
537	/// *library*, that object becomes one member of the crate's `.rlib`
538	/// archive, and linkers only extract an archive member when it defines a
539	/// symbol that some already-linked object references. The backend defines
540	/// a global anchor symbol inside the artifact object for exactly this
541	/// purpose; here we emit the matching reference. Reading the anchor's
542	/// address through `black_box` inside `load_named()` means that any
543	/// program calling `load()` carries an undefined reference to the anchor,
544	/// which forces the linker to pull the artifact member out of the rlib.
545	/// Without this handshake the bundle was silently dropped and `load()`
546	/// failed at runtime with `ModuleNotFound` (issue #72).
547	///
548	/// Without an owner filter, both sides keep using the legacy package+version
549	/// anchor for compatibility with older wrappers and backends. A non-empty
550	/// owner filter activates the v2 package+version+crate+binary identity. That
551	/// target-specific identity prevents an unselected binary from satisfying a
552	/// selected library's reference (or vice versa); an unselected new macro emits
553	/// no reference at all. The backend also keeps a weak legacy alias for older
554	/// macro expansions in mixed-version builds.
555	///
556	/// The reference is only emitted when the module is guaranteed to produce
557	/// an artifact for this crate. Generic kernels are monomorphized (and
558	/// their PTX embedded) in the *consuming* crate, so a module with only
559	/// generic kernels yields no artifact here, and an anchor reference would
560	/// be an undefined-symbol link error. The same reasoning extends to
561	/// `#[cfg]`-gated kernels: the anchor is guarded by the disjunction of
562	/// the kernels' cfg conditions so it is only referenced when at least one
563	/// concrete kernel is actually compiled.
564	fn cuda_module_artifact_anchor_statements(
565	    kernels: &[CudaModuleKernel],
566	) -> syn::Result<TokenStream2> {
567	    let (Ok(package_name), Ok(package_version), Ok(crate_name)) = (
568	        std::env::var("CARGO_PKG_NAME"),
569	        std::env::var("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"),
570	        std::env::var("CARGO_CRATE_NAME"),
571	    ) else {
572	        // Not built by cargo (e.g. a raw rustc invocation): the backend
573	        // falls back to crate-name-based bundle naming and we cannot
574	        // reproduce it exactly, so skip the anchor rather than risk an
575	        // undefined symbol.
576	        return Ok(TokenStream2::new());
577	    };
578	
579	    let owner_filter = std::env::var("CUDA_OXIDE_DEVICE_CODEGEN_CRATE").ok();
580	    let owner_selection = device_codegen_owner_selection(owner_filter.as_deref(), &crate_name);
581	    if owner_selection == Some(false) {
582	        // The backend deliberately omits this crate's artifact. Omitting the
583	        // reference as well keeps the host link valid without pretending that
584	        // a loadable bundle exists.
585	        return Ok(TokenStream2::new());
586	    }
587	
588	    let non_generic: Vec<&CudaModuleKernel> =
589	        kernels.iter().filter(|kernel| !kernel.is_generic).collect();
590	    if non_generic.is_empty() {
591	        return Ok(TokenStream2::new());
592	    }
593	
594	    let cfg_guard = if non_generic.iter().any(|kernel| kernel.cfg_attrs.is_empty()) {
595	        // At least one concrete kernel is compiled unconditionally, so the
596	        // artifact object always exists: no guard needed.
597	        None
598	    } else {
599	        // Every concrete kernel is cfg-gated. Reference the anchor only
600	        // when at least one of them is enabled. A kernel with several cfg
601	        // attributes requires all of them, hence all(...) per kernel
602	        // joined under any(...).
603	        let alternatives = non_generic
604	            .iter()
605	            .map(|kernel| {
606	                let predicates = kernel
607	                    .cfg_attrs
608	                    .iter()
609	                    .map(|attr| attr.parse_args::<TokenStream2>())
610	                    .collect::<syn::Result<Vec<_>>>()?;
611	                Ok(quote! { all( #(#predicates),* ) })
612	            })
613	            .collect::<syn::Result<Vec<_>>>()?;
614	        Some(quote! { #[cfg(any( #(#alternatives),* ))] })
615	    };
616	
617	    let binary_name = std::env::var("CARGO_BIN_NAME").ok();
618	    let anchor = if owner_selection.is_some() {
619	        artifact_anchor_symbol_v2(
620	            &package_name,
621	            &package_version,
622	            &crate_name,
623	            binary_name.as_deref(),
624	        )
625	    } else {
626	        artifact_anchor_symbol(&package_name, &package_version)
627	    };
628	    let anchor_name = LitStr::new(&anchor, proc_macro2::Span::call_site());
629	    Ok(quote! {
630	        // Keep-alive handshake with the codegen backend: see the macro
631	        // crate's `cuda_module_artifact_anchor_statements` for details.
632	        #cfg_guard
633	        let _artifact_anchor: *const ::core::primitive::u8 = {
634	            unsafe extern "C" {
635	                #[link_name = #anchor_name]
636	                static CUDA_OXIDE_BUNDLE_ANCHOR: ::core::primitive::u8;
637	            }
638	            ::std::hint::black_box(unsafe {
639	                ::core::ptr::addr_of!(CUDA_OXIDE_BUNDLE_ANCHOR)
640	            })
641	        };
642	    })
643	}
644	
645	fn device_codegen_owner_selection(raw: Option<&str>, crate_name: &str) -> Option<bool> {
646	    let crate_name = crate_name.trim().replace('-', "_");
647	    raw.and_then(|raw| {
648	        let owners: Vec<_> = raw
649	            .split(',')
650	            .map(|name| name.trim().replace('-', "_"))
651	            .filter(|name| !name.is_empty())
652	            .collect();
653	        (!owners.is_empty()).then(|| owners.iter().any(|owner| owner == &crate_name))
654	    })
655	}

657	/// A `#[constant]` static collected from a `#[cuda_module]` body.
658	struct CudaModuleConstant {
659	    ident: Ident,
660	    ty: Box<Type>,
661	    cfg_attrs: Vec<syn::Attribute>,
662	    method_attrs: Vec<syn::Attribute>,
663	    symbol: String,
664	}
665	
666	fn collect_cuda_module_constants(
667	    items: &[Item],
668	    module_ident: &Ident,
669	) -> syn::Result<Vec<CudaModuleConstant>> {
670	    let mut constants = Vec::new();
671	    for item in items {
672	        let Item::Static(item_static) = item else {
673	            continue;
674	        };
675	        if !has_attr_named(&item_static.attrs, "constant") {
676	            continue;
677	        }
678	        if extract_constant_inner_ty(&item_static.ty).is_none() {
679	            return Err(syn::Error::new_spanned(
680	                &item_static.ty,
681	                concat!(
682	                    "#[constant] static must have type `ConstantMemory<T>` ",
683	                    "(e.g. `static FOO: ConstantMemory<[f32; 4]> = ConstantMemory::UNINIT;`). ",
684	                    "The wrapper prevents the compiler from constant-folding the initializer into kernel bodies.",
685	                ),
686	            ));
687	        }
688	        constants.push(CudaModuleConstant {
689	            ident: item_static.ident.clone(),
690	            ty: item_static.ty.clone(),
691	            cfg_attrs: cuda_module_cfg_attrs(&item_static.attrs),
692	            method_attrs: cuda_module_method_attrs(&item_static.attrs),
693	            symbol: cuda_module_constant_symbol(module_ident, &item_static.ident),
694	        });
695	    }
696	    Ok(constants)
697	}

699	fn cuda_module_constant_symbol(module_ident: &Ident, ident: &Ident) -> String {
700	    let start = ident.span().start();
701	    let base = format!(
702	        "{}_L{}C{}_{}",
703	        module_ident, start.line, start.column, ident
704	    );
705	    constant_symbol(&base)
706	}
707	
708	fn cuda_module_items_with_constant_symbols(
709	    items: &[Item],
710	    constants: &[CudaModuleConstant],
711	) -> Vec<TokenStream2> {
712	    let mut constants = constants.iter();
713	    items
714	        .iter()
715	        .map(|item| {
716	            let Item::Static(item_static) = item else {
717	                return quote! { #item };
718	            };
719	            if !has_attr_named(&item_static.attrs, "constant") {
720	                return quote! { #item };
721	            }
722	            let constant = constants
723	                .next()
724	                .expect("constant collection and rewrite order drifted");
725	
726	            let mut item_static = item_static.clone();
727	            let symbol = LitStr::new(&constant.symbol, constant.ident.span());
728	            item_static.attrs = item_static
729	                .attrs
730	                .into_iter()
731	                .map(|attr| {
732	                    if attr_path_ends_with(&attr, "constant") {
733	                        let path = attr.path().clone();
734	                        parse_quote!(#[#path(export_name = #symbol)])
735	                    } else {
736	                        attr
737	                    }
738	                })
739	                .collect();
740	            quote! { #item_static }
741	        })
742	        .collect()
743	}

745	fn cuda_module_constant_field_ident(ident: &Ident) -> Ident {
746	    format_ident!("__{}", ident)
747	}

749	fn cuda_module_constant_resolver_ident(ident: &Ident) -> Ident {
750	    format_ident!("__resolve_{}", ident)

753	/// Extract `T` from a `ConstantMemory<T>` type path. Returns `None` for anything
754	/// that's not a path ending in `ConstantMemory<...>` with one generic argument.
755	fn extract_constant_inner_ty(ty: &Type) -> Option<&Type> {
756	    let Type::Path(type_path) = ty else {
757	        return None;
758	    };
759	    let last = type_path.path.segments.last()?;
760	    if last.ident != "ConstantMemory" {
761	        return None;
762	    }
763	    let syn::PathArguments::AngleBracketed(args) = &last.arguments else {
764	        return None;
765	    };
766	    if args.args.len() != 1 {
767	        return None;
768	    }
769	    args.args.iter().find_map(|a| match a {
770	        syn::GenericArgument::Type(t) => Some(t),
771	        _ => None,
772	    })
773	}
774	
775	/// Like [`extract_constant_inner_ty`] but for sites that have already been
776	/// gated by `#[constant]`'s type-path validation, so extraction failure is
777	/// a compiler-internal invariant violation, not a user error.
778	fn constant_inner_ty(ty: &Type) -> &Type {
779	    extract_constant_inner_ty(ty).unwrap_or_else(|| {
780	        panic!(
781	            "#[cuda_module] collected a #[constant] static whose type is not ConstantMemory<T>; \
782	             this should have been rejected by the #[constant] attribute"
783	        )
784	    })
785	}

787	fn generate_cuda_module_constant_field(constant: &CudaModuleConstant) -> TokenStream2 {
788	    let CudaModuleConstant {
789	        ident, cfg_attrs, ..
790	    } = constant;
791	    let field = cuda_module_constant_field_ident(ident);
792	    quote! {
793	        #(#cfg_attrs)*
794	        #field: ::std::sync::Arc<::std::sync::Mutex<::core::option::Option<::cuda_core::ConstantHandle>>>,
795	    }
796	}
797	
798	fn generate_cuda_module_constant_initializer(constant: &CudaModuleConstant) -> TokenStream2 {
799	    let CudaModuleConstant {
800	        ident, cfg_attrs, ..
801	    } = constant;
802	    let field = cuda_module_constant_field_ident(ident);
803	    quote! {
804	        #(#cfg_attrs)*
805	        #field: ::std::sync::Arc::new(::std::sync::Mutex::new(::core::option::Option::None)),
806	    }
807	}
808	
809	fn generate_cuda_module_constant_resolver_method(constant: &CudaModuleConstant) -> TokenStream2 {
810	    let CudaModuleConstant {
811	        ident,
812	        ty,
813	        cfg_attrs,
814	        symbol,
815	        ..
816	    } = constant;
817	    let field = cuda_module_constant_field_ident(ident);
818	    let resolver = cuda_module_constant_resolver_ident(ident);
819	    let symbol_lit = LitStr::new(symbol, ident.span());
820	    let inner_ty = constant_inner_ty(ty);
821	    let mismatch_msg = format!(
822	        "host/device size mismatch for #[constant] static `{ident}`: \
823	         device size {{}} bytes, host expected {{}} bytes (PTX symbol `{symbol}`)"
824	    );
825	    quote! {
826	        #(#cfg_attrs)*
827	        #[allow(non_snake_case)]
828	        fn #resolver(&self) -> ::core::result::Result<::cuda_core::ConstantHandle, ::cuda_core::DriverError> {
829	            let mut slot = self
830	                .#field
831	                .lock()
832	                .expect("cuda constant handle cache mutex poisoned");
833	            if let ::core::option::Option::Some(handle) = *slot {
834	                return ::core::result::Result::Ok(handle);
835	            }
836	
837	            let (dptr, size) = self.__module.get_global(#symbol_lit)?;
838	            assert_eq!(
839	                size,
840	                ::core::mem::size_of::<#inner_ty>(),
841	                #mismatch_msg,
842	                size,
843	                ::core::mem::size_of::<#inner_ty>(),
844	            );
845	            // SAFETY: `dptr` was just resolved by `cuModuleGetGlobal` for a
846	            // module that the LoadedModule keeps alive, and the size matches
847	            // `size_of::<#inner_ty>()` (asserted above).
848	            let handle = unsafe { ::cuda_core::ConstantHandle::from_raw(dptr) };
849	            *slot = ::core::option::Option::Some(handle);
850	            ::core::result::Result::Ok(handle)
851	        }
852	    }
853	}

855	/// Generate stream-ordered `set_<name>` and one-shot `set_<name>_blocking`
856	/// methods on `LoadedModule`. The async setter stages owned host bytes so
857	/// temporaries remain valid until CUDA reaches the stream callback.
858	fn generate_cuda_module_set_constant_method(constant: &CudaModuleConstant) -> TokenStream2 {
859	    let CudaModuleConstant {
860	        ident,
861	        ty,
862	        cfg_attrs,
863	        method_attrs,
864	        ..
865	    } = constant;
866	    let method_suffix = ident.to_string().to_ascii_lowercase();
867	    let method_name = format_ident!("set_{}", method_suffix);
868	    let blocking_name = format_ident!("set_{}_blocking", method_suffix);
869	    let resolver = cuda_module_constant_resolver_ident(ident);
870	    let inner_ty = constant_inner_ty(ty);
871	
872	    quote! {
873	        #(#cfg_attrs)*
874	        #(#method_attrs)*
875	        #[allow(non_snake_case)]
876	        pub fn #method_name(
877	            &self,
878	            stream: &::cuda_core::CudaStream,
879	            value: &#inner_ty,
880	        ) -> ::core::result::Result<(), ::cuda_core::DriverError> {
881	            let handle = self.#resolver()?;
882	            let num_bytes = ::core::mem::size_of::<#inner_ty>();
883	            let mut bytes = ::std::boxed::Box::<[u8]>::new_uninit_slice(num_bytes);
884	            unsafe {
885	                ::core::ptr::copy_nonoverlapping(
886	                    value as *const #inner_ty as *const u8,
887	                    bytes.as_mut_ptr() as *mut u8,
888	                    num_bytes,
889	                );
890	            }
891	            handle.write_async_staged(stream, bytes)
892	        }
893	
894	        #(#cfg_attrs)*
895	        #(#method_attrs)*
896	        #[allow(non_snake_case)]
897	        pub fn #blocking_name(
898	            &self,
899	            value: &#inner_ty,
900	        ) -> ::core::result::Result<(), ::cuda_core::DriverError> {
901	            let handle = self.#resolver()?;
902	            // SAFETY: handle was size-checked against `#inner_ty` by the lazy
903	            // resolver; `value` is a valid host pointer for
904	            // `size_of::<#inner_ty>()`.
905	            unsafe {
906	                handle.write_blocking(
907	                    &self.__module,
908	                    value as *const #inner_ty as *const u8,
909	                    ::core::mem::size_of::<#inner_ty>(),
910	                )
911	            }
912	        }
913	    }
914	}

1001	fn cuda_module_host_type(
1002	    ty: &Type,
1003	) -> syn::Result<(TokenStream2, TokenStream2, CudaModuleParamMarshal)> {
1004	    let async_lifetime = cuda_module_async_lifetime();
1005	    if let Some((elem_ty, mutable)) = cuda_module_slice_elem(ty) {
1006	        let sync_host_ty = if mutable {
1007	            quote! { &mut ::cuda_core::DeviceBuffer<#elem_ty> }
1008	        } else {
1009	            quote! { &::cuda_core::DeviceBuffer<#elem_ty> }
1010	        };
1011	        let (async_host_ty, marshal) = if mutable {
1012	            (
1013	                quote! { &#async_lifetime mut impl ::cuda_host::KernelSliceArgMut<Elem = #elem_ty> },
1014	                CudaModuleParamMarshal::WritableDeviceBuffer {
1015	                    elem_ty: quote! { #elem_ty },
1016	                },
1017	            )
1018	        } else {
1019	            (
1020	                quote! { &#async_lifetime impl ::cuda_host::KernelSliceArg<Elem = #elem_ty> },
1021	                CudaModuleParamMarshal::ReadOnlyDeviceBuffer {
1022	                    elem_ty: quote! { #elem_ty },
1023	                },
1024	            )
1025	        };
1026	        return Ok((sync_host_ty, async_host_ty, marshal));
1027	    }
1028	
1029	    if let Some(elem_ty) = cuda_module_disjoint_slice_elem(ty) {
1030	        return Ok((
1031	            quote! { &mut ::cuda_core::DeviceBuffer<#elem_ty> },
1032	            quote! { &#async_lifetime mut impl ::cuda_host::KernelSliceArgMut<Elem = #elem_ty> },
1033	            CudaModuleParamMarshal::WritableDeviceBuffer {
1034	                elem_ty: quote! { #elem_ty },
1035	            },
1036	        ));
1037	    }
1038	
1039	    if matches!(ty, Type::Reference(_)) {
1040	        return Err(syn::Error::new_spanned(
1041	            ty,
1042	            "cuda_module only supports slice references; use &[T], &mut [T], DisjointSlice<T>, a raw pointer, or a by-value KernelScalar",
1043	        ));
1044	    }
1045	
1046	    Ok((
1047	        quote! { #ty },
1048	        quote! { #ty },
1049	        CudaModuleParamMarshal::Scalar,
1050	    ))
1051	}

1053	fn cuda_module_slice_elem(ty: &Type) -> Option<(TokenStream2, bool)> {
1054	    let Type::Reference(type_ref) = ty else {
1055	        return None;
1056	    };
1057	    let Type::Slice(slice) = &*type_ref.elem else {
1058	        return None;
1059	    };
1060	    let elem = &slice.elem;
1061	    Some((quote! { #elem }, type_ref.mutability.is_some()))
1062	}
1063	
1064	fn cuda_module_disjoint_slice_elem(ty: &Type) -> Option<TokenStream2> {
1065	    let Type::Path(type_path) = ty else {
1066	        return None;
1067	    };
1068	    let segment = type_path.path.segments.last()?;
1069	    if segment.ident != "DisjointSlice" {
1070	        return None;
1071	    }
1072	    let PathArguments::AngleBracketed(args) = &segment.arguments else {
1073	        return None;
1074	    };
1075	    args.args.iter().find_map(|arg| {
1076	        if let GenericArgument::Type(ty) = arg {
1077	            Some(quote! { #ty })
1078	        } else {
1079	            None
1080	        }
1081	    })
1082	}

1284	            }
1285	        };
1286	        generics.make_where_clause().predicates.push(predicate);
1287	    }
1288	    for param in &kernel.params {
1289	        if matches!(param.marshal, CudaModuleParamMarshal::Scalar) {
1290	            let host_ty = &param.async_host_ty;
1291	            generics
1292	                .make_where_clause()
1293	                .predicates
1294	                .push(syn::parse_quote! { #host_ty: ::cuda_host::KernelScalar + 'static });
1295	        }
1296	    }
1297	    generics
1298	}

1368	            let len_name = internal_ident(&format!("__cuda_oxide_arg_{index}_len"));
1369	            quote! {
1370	                let (mut #ptr_name, mut #len_name) =
1371	                    ::cuda_host::read_only_device_buffer_arg(#name);
1372	                ::cuda_host::push_kernel_device_slice(
1373	                    &mut #args,
1374	                    &mut #ptr_name,
1375	                    &mut #len_name,
1376	                );
1377	            }
1378	        }
1379	        CudaModuleParamMarshal::WritableDeviceBuffer { .. } => {

1382	            quote! {

1439	fn cuda_module_function_binding(kernel: &CudaModuleKernel) -> TokenStream2 {
1440	    let function = internal_ident("__cuda_oxide_function");
1441	    if kernel.is_generic {
1442	        let fn_name = &kernel.fn_name;
1443	        let ptx_name_fn = format_ident!("{}_ptx_name", fn_name);
1444	        let codegen_args = codegen_generic_arguments(&kernel.generics);
1445	        let turbofish = if codegen_args.is_empty() {
1446	            quote! {}
1447	        } else {
1448	            quote! { ::<#(#codegen_args),*> }
1449	        };
1450	        let ptx_name = internal_ident("__cuda_oxide_ptx_name");
1451	        let function_storage = internal_ident("__cuda_oxide_function_storage");
1452	        let cache = internal_ident("__cuda_oxide_function_cache");
1453	        quote! {
1454	            let #ptx_name = #ptx_name_fn #turbofish ();
1455	            let #function_storage = {
1456	                let mut #cache = self
1457	                    .__generic_functions
1458	                    .lock()
1459	                    .expect("cuda_module generic function cache poisoned");
1460	                if let Some(#function) = #cache.get(#ptx_name) {
1461	                    #function.clone()
1462	                } else {
1463	                    let #function = self.__module.load_function(#ptx_name)?;
1464	                    #cache.insert(#ptx_name, #function.clone());
1465	                    #function
1466	                }
1467	            };
1468	            let #function = &#function_storage;
1469	        }
1470	    } else {
1471	        let field = cuda_module_function_field(&kernel.fn_name);
1472	        quote! {
1473	            let #function = &self.#field;
1474	        }
1475	    }
1476	}

1538	/// Whether rustc must create distinct code for this generic parameter list.
1539	///
1540	/// Lifetimes are deliberately excluded: rustc erases them before codegen, so
1541	/// they cannot distinguish PTX entry points. Type and const parameters both
1542	/// participate in monomorphization and therefore in kernel identity.
1543	fn has_codegen_generics(generics: &syn::Generics) -> bool {
1544	    generics
1545	        .params
1546	        .iter()
1547	        .any(|param| matches!(param, GenericParam::Type(_) | GenericParam::Const(_)))

1550	/// Ordered generic arguments accepted by a function turbofish.
1551	///
1552	/// Rust function turbofish syntax omits lifetime arguments. Type and const
1553	/// identifiers must remain in declaration order so mixed `<T, const N>`
1554	/// kernels instantiate the exact function item requested by the caller.
1555	fn codegen_generic_arguments(generics: &syn::Generics) -> Vec<TokenStream2> {
1556	    generics
1557	        .params
1558	        .iter()
1559	        .filter_map(|param| match param {
1560	            GenericParam::Type(type_param) => {
1561	                let ident = &type_param.ident;
1562	                Some(quote! { #ident })
1563	            }
1564	            GenericParam::Const(const_param) => {
1565	                let ident = &const_param.ident;
1566	                Some(quote! { #ident })
1567	            }
1568	            GenericParam::Lifetime(_) => None,
1569	        })
1570	        .collect()
1571	}

1573	/// Ordered arguments for applying a generated marker type.
1574	///
1575	/// Unlike function turbofish syntax, a type application retains lifetime
1576	/// parameters. The specialization hash still ignores their identity because
1577	/// rustc's `TypeId` pipeline erases regions.
1578	fn generic_arguments(generics: &syn::Generics) -> Vec<TokenStream2> {
1579	    generics
1580	        .params
1581	        .iter()
1582	        .map(|param| match param {
1583	            GenericParam::Lifetime(lifetime_param) => {
1584	                let lifetime = &lifetime_param.lifetime;
1585	                quote! { #lifetime }
1586	            }
1587	            GenericParam::Type(type_param) => {
1588	                let ident = &type_param.ident;
1589	                quote! { #ident }
1590	            }
1591	            GenericParam::Const(const_param) => {
1592	                let ident = &const_param.ident;
1593	                quote! { #ident }
1594	            }
1595	        })
1596	        .collect()
1597	}

1599	/// Types used by `PhantomData` so generated marker types correctly witness
1600	/// every lifetime and type parameter. Const parameters are part of the marker
1601	/// type by construction and need no field-level witness.
1602	fn generic_phantom_type(generics: &syn::Generics) -> TokenStream2 {
1603	    let witnesses: Vec<TokenStream2> = generics
1604	        .params
1605	        .iter()
1606	        .filter_map(|param| match param {
1607	            GenericParam::Lifetime(lifetime_param) => {
1608	                let lifetime = &lifetime_param.lifetime;
1609	                Some(quote! { &#lifetime () })
1610	            }
1611	            GenericParam::Type(type_param) => {
1612	                let ident = &type_param.ident;
1613	                Some(quote! { *const #ident })
1614	            }
1615	            GenericParam::Const(_) => None,
1616	        })
1617	        .collect();
1618	
1619	    if witnesses.is_empty() {
1620	        quote! { () }
1621	    } else {
1622	        quote! { (#(#witnesses,)*) }
1623	    }
1624	}
